When a new user signs up on a website, we typically have limited information about them - no interaction with items, no user profile, and no social links with other users. Under such circumstances, we still expect our recommender systems to attract users at the first time so that the users decide to stay on the website and become active users. This problem falls into the new user cold-start category, and it is crucial to the development and even survival of a company. Existing works on user cold-start recommendation often require additional user efforts, for instance, setting up an interview process, or making use of side information, such as user demographics, locations, social relations, etc. However, users may not be willing to take the interview and side information on cold-start users is usually not available.

In this paper, we study the landscape of the population negative log-likelihood function of Gaussian mixture models with a general number of components. Due to nonconvexity, there exist multiple local minima that are not globally optimal, even when the mixture is well-separated. We show that all local minima share the same form of structure that partially identifies the component centers of the true mixture, in the sense that each local minimum involves a non-overlapping combination of fitting multiple Gaussians to a single true component and fitting a single Gaussian to multiple true components. Our results apply to the setting where the true mixture components satisfy a certain separation condition, and are valid even when the number of components is over- or under-specified. For Gaussian mixtures with three components, we obtain sharper results in terms of the scaling with the separation between the components.

Sid Meier's Civilization  Beyond Earth is a turn-based game that challenges you to build your civilization to achieve a specific goal or 'victory' on a new planet. Unlike previous games in the Civilization series, this installment takes a bold approach by setting the game in the future, allowing players to colonize a new planet and navigate its unique challenges. One of the most innovative features of this game is the introduction of the affinity system and orbital layer, which adds a new layer of depth to the gameplay experience. However, some players may find the game overwhelming at times, particularly with the sheer amount of new mechanics introduced. To further enhance the game, it would be beneficial to incorporate more diverse and visually stunning artwork, such as the environment, to create a more immersive experience when interacting with other leaders. This could include the ability to explore and experience the environment in a more engaging and visually appealing way.

Sustainability concerns itself with the specifics of what is caught, where it is caught, and how it is caught. However, I am more interested in understanding the people involved—their motivations and their stories. I care about the individuals who contribute to my dining experience and how my choices affect their lives and vice versa. I want to learn why they fish and how they depend on the ocean's resources for their livelihoods. By gaining this understanding, we can transform our view of seafood from just a consumable product to an opportunity to rejuvenate our ecosystems. It allows us to appreciate the seafood we are lucky enough to consume. So, what do we call this approach? I believe it is best described as "restorative seafood." While sustainability focuses on enduring and maintaining, restoration emphasizes replenishing and advancing.

British Stars Missed Out on Major Oscars

Hopes for British success at the Oscars were disappointed as UK stars went home empty-handed in the acting and directing categories. Despite receiving three nominations, Mike Leigh's "Vera Drake" did not clinch the awards for either Best Director or Best Screenplay, and lead actress Imelda Staunton also went unrecognized. Kate Winslet was bested by Hilary Swank for Best Actress, and both Clive Owen and Sophie Okonedo fell short in their bids for Best Supporting Actor. However, Andrea Arnold from the UK emerged victorious, winning the Short Film Award for her gripping drama, "Wasp." Reflecting on her win, Arnold, who was a presenter on children's television shows "Number 73" and "Motormouth" in the 1980s, described the experience as "totally overwhelming." "Wasp," which tells the story of a single mother struggling to get by, triumphed over entries by fellow UK nominees Gary McKendry and Ashvin Kumar.

Reasoning about the evolution of graphs over time is a complex concept in various fields, such as bioinformatics, physics, and social networks. We focus on a common scenario where edges represent either short-term interactions (like messaging) or long-term structural connections (such as friendships). Typically, long-term edges are determined by humans, which can be costly to produce and may not be optimal for downstream tasks. To address these challenges, we introduce a model that leverages temporal point processes and variational autoencoders to infer temporal attention between nodes by observing their communication. By learning this temporal attention through the dynamics of node interactions, we enhance flexibility in feature propagation between nodes and simultaneously bypass the limitations associated with human-defined edges.

**Subject:** Urgent Concern Regarding PX Day-Ahead Auction Disruptions Due to ISO Rule Changes  

**Date:** Monday, December 11, 2000  
**To:** All PX Market Participants  
**From:** John Yurkanin, Chief Operating Officer  

Dear Market Participants,  

The California ISO's emergency tariff filing last Friday has significantly disrupted the PX Day-Ahead Auction market. The simultaneous implementation of soft and hard caps has created a fundamental disconnect, exposing all participants to unacceptable risks. Furthermore, these changes are unlikely to achieve the ISO's intended reduction in real-time energy sales and procurement.  

As a result, supply resources will continue prioritizing the ISO's higher-priced real-time opportunities. To address this issue, the PX is formally requesting that Terry Winter of the ISO authorize immediate adjustment bids for all submitted schedules at market-relevant prices.  

We appreciate your attention to this critical matter and will keep you informed of further developments.  

Best regards,  
John Yurkanin  
Chief Operating Officer

What is a parent? What is a parent? It's not an easy question. Today, we have adoption, stepfamilies, surrogate mothers. Many parents face tough questions and tough decisions. Shall we tell our child about the sperm donation? If so, when? What words to use? Sperm donors are often referred to as "biological fathers," but should we really be using the word "father"? As a philosopher and social scientist, I have been studying these questions about the concept of parenthood. But today, I will talk to you about what I learned from talking to parents and children. I will show you that they know what matters most in a family, even though their family looks a little different.

In 1903, the President of the United States embarked on a three-day camping trip in California's Yosemite Valley. President Theodore Roosevelt slept among a grove of towering sequoia trees, endured a snowstorm, and spent hours engaging in lively conversation around the campfire with his host and guide, conservationist John Muir. Roosevelt, an ardent lover of the outdoors, was invited by Muir for more than just a camping trip; Yosemite was in danger of overdevelopment. Although the valley was protected land since 1864, it was still at risk of being overrun by developers in 1903. This was the culmination of a long-standing struggle to strike a balance between preservation and public use – two goals that proved challenging to achieve. The battle for Yosemite began in 1849 with the gold rush, when miners flocked west in search of gold in the Sierra Nevada foothills.

Routing controllability of autonomous vehicles (AVs) has been shown to reduce the impact of selfish routing on network efficiency. However, the assumption that AVs would readily allow themselves to be controlled externally by a central agency is unrealistic. In this paper, we propose a joint routing and pricing control scheme that aims to incentivize AVs to seek centrally controlled system optimal (SO) routing by saving on tolls, while user equilibrium (UE) seeking AVs and human-driven vehicles (HVs) are subject to a congestion charge. The problem is formulated as a bi-level optimization, where dynamic tolls are optimized in the upper level, while the lower level is a mixed equilibrium simulation-based dynamic traffic assignment model considering a mixed fleet of AVs and HVs.

Continuous-time quantum walks have been proven to be an extremely useful framework for the design of several quantum algorithms. Often, the running time of quantum algorithms in this framework is characterized by the quantum hitting time  the time required for the quantum walk to find a vertex of interest with a high probability. In this article, we provide improved upper bounds for the quantum hitting time that can be applied to several continuous-time quantum walk-based quantum algorithms. In particular, we apply our techniques to the glued-trees problem, improving their hitting time upper bound by a polynomial factor  from O(n^5) to O(n^2 log n). Moreover, our methods also enable exponentially improved dependence on precision of the continuous-time quantum walk-based algorithm to find a marked node on any ergodic, reversible Markov chain by Chakraborty et al.

Last night was the worst. I threw away the sweater I was knitting. I only had about 14 inches left. I lost probably $10 worth of yarn (it's $8 a skein). Ranking algorithms play a crucial role in online platforms, from search engines to recommender systems. In this paper, we uncover a surprising consequence of popularity-based rankings: the fewer items reporting a given signal, the higher the share of overall traffic they collectively attract. This phenomenon, known as the few-get-richer effect, emerges in settings with few distinct classes of items (e.g., left-leaning news sources versus right-leaning news sources) and when rankings are based on popularity. We demonstrate analytically that the effect arises when people tend to click on top-ranked items and have diverse preferences for the classes of items. Through simulations and an online experiment with human participants, we investigate how the strength of the effect varies with assumptions about the setting and user behavior. Our findings have significant implications for understanding the dissemination of misinformation.

This paper examines multi-agent discrete-event systems under partial observation. The agents can be categorized into multiple groups, where agents within each group possess similar (isomorphic) state transition structures, allowing them to be relabeled into a common template. Leveraging this template, we design a scalable supervisor whose state size and computational complexity remain unaffected by the number of agents, even under partial observation. Notably, this scalable supervisor maintains its validity without recomputation, regardless of agent additions or removals from the system. We extend our previous findings to partial observation by establishing sufficient conditions for safety and maximal permissiveness of the scalable least restrictive supervisor at the template level. An illustrative example demonstrates the proposed scalable supervisory synthesis approach.

As the clock ticks down to the midnight deadline on February 1, Fiat and General Motors (GM) are racing against time to resolve their disagreement over a potential takeover. The 2000 agreement, which granted Fiat the right to sell its car division to GM, is now at risk of expiring. GM, struggling to stem losses in its European operations, has reconsidered its interest in acquiring the unprofitable Fiat unit. The stalemate in talks has sent Fiat's shares plummeting 1.2% on Tuesday, following a 4% surge on Monday on hopes of a deal being reached. GM is reportedly offering approximately $2 billion (£1.06 billion) to extricate itself from the arrangement, citing Fiat's decision to divest its finance arm and reduce GM's stake through a capital-raising initiative as grounds for nullifying the original agreement. The 2000 deal was forged in a high-stakes game of musical chairs, with GM and DaimlerChrysler vying for a partnership with Fiat.

When a new user signs up on a website, we typically have no information about them, i.e., no interaction with items, no user profile, and no social links with other users. Under such circumstances, we still expect our recommender systems to attract users at the first encounter, encouraging them to stay on the website and become active users. This problem falls into the new user cold-start category, which is crucial to a company's development and even survival. Existing works on user cold-start recommendation either require additional user efforts, such as setting up an interview process, or utilize side information, such as user demographics, locations, and social relations. However, users may be unwilling to participate in an interview, and side information on cold-start users is usually not available.

I think this Assassin's Creed nailed the mechanics on all fronts (ship battles, sailing, hunting (fish), parkour, etc.). I felt much more attached to this character as well. The story starts out extremely rough but does get much, much better as you play (I think this is true of every AC game personally). Edward was quite the interesting character, and so were all the side characters. I felt like all the collectibles actually meant something in this AC game, unlike any of the previous ones, and maybe it’s because they had a bigger purpose in the game. They definitely were not required, but they were fun to get, and you were rewarded in a very balanced way.

Extreme mass ratio inspirals (EMRIs) can be classified as dry EMRIs and wet EMRIs based on their formation mechanisms. Dry (or the "loss-cone") EMRIs, previously considered as the main EMRI sources for the laser interferometer space antenna, are primarily produced by multi-body scattering in the nuclear star cluster and gravitational capture. This letter highlights an alternative EMRI formation channel  wet EMRI formation assisted by the accretion flow around accreting galactic-center massive black holes (MBHs). In this channel, the accretion disk captures stellar-mass black holes that are initially moving on inclined orbits, and subsequently drives them to migrate towards the MBH - this process boosts the formation rate of EMRIs in such galaxies by orders of magnitude.

The Carbon Cycle: Tracing Humanity's Footprint  

Carbon is a fundamental element that circulates continuously through Earth's natural systems—embedded in living organisms, dissolved in ocean waters, suspended in the atmosphere, and locked within terrestrial and subterranean rock formations. This perpetual cycle sees carbon released back into the air when organic matter decomposes, only to be reabsorbed by new plant growth.  

However, across geological timescales, a fraction of this carbon—originally stored in ancient forests and marine life—transforms into sedimentary rock or fossilized deposits like coal, oil, and natural gas. While volcanic activity naturally liberates trace amounts of this sequestered carbon, human industrialization has dramatically accelerated the process. Through fossil fuel combustion, we now emit approximately sixty times more carbon annually than all volcanic eruptions combined—disrupting a balance refined over millennia.

This paper demonstrates that the eccentric debris rings surrounding the stars Fomalhaut and HD 202628 are narrower than expected in the standard eccentric planet perturbation scenario, often referred to as the "pericenter glow" phenomenon. The standard scenario postulates an initially circular and narrow belt of planetesimals at semi-major axis a, whose eccentricity is increased to ef after the gas disc has dispersed by secular perturbations from an eccentric planet, resulting in a belt of width 2ae_f. In a minor modification of this scenario, narrower belts can arise if the planetesimals are initially eccentric, which could result from earlier planet perturbations during the gas-rich protoplanetary disc phase. Alternatively, a primordial eccentricity could be caused by instabilities that increase the disc eccentricity, without the need for any planets. This alternative scenario highlights the potential for the formation of narrow debris rings in the early stages of planetary system formation.

Hello. This is my first journey outside the confines of Gaza—I have never ventured beyond its walls until now. I am thrilled to be here. (applause) My lifelong dream has been to become a pilot, to fly an airplane, and to experience the freedom of soaring through the sky. Unfortunately, that dream hasn't come true. Living in Gaza means there is no airport, and all borders are sealed off in every direction. We exist in what is one of the largest open-air prisons worldwide. My only option is to gaze up at the sky. There are days when we are fortunate if we have electricity for only four or five hours. When the weather turns chilly, we gather to build a fire either in front of or atop our homes.

It has been humorously remarked that politics is essentially "showbiz for ugly people." Based on that notion, I feel like I've truly made it. Additionally, it’s worth considering the privilege it is, as a politician, to deliver a TED Talk, particularly in the U.K., where politics' reputation has plummeted due to the expenses scandal. There was even a recent story suggesting that scientists contemplated substituting rats in their experiments with politicians. When asked why, they responded, "Well, there's an abundant supply of politicians, nobody really objects to what happens to them, and, after all, there are some things that rats simply won't do." (laughter) Now, knowing your appreciation for data, I’m commencing with a slide rich with it.

Citizens of the world, we confront a global crisis of unparalleled magnitude. Please stand by for an urgent message from... the Secretary-General of the United Nations, António Guterres.  

The climate emergency represents the most critical challenge of our era. We are racing against time—and falling behind. Across the globe, frustration mounts, particularly among the youth, over insufficient action. Greater ambition is demanded from all: governments, cities, businesses, investors, and individuals alike.  

That is why I welcome the launch of TED Countdown. Your innovation and influence can propel us toward a carbon-neutral world by 2050—our only path to preventing the most catastrophic effects of climate change. The solutions exist: we possess the science, the tools, and the means. Now, we must channel political will and collective energy to rise to this challenge.

Kevin Spacey, 45, attended the British Premiere of the new film, "Beyond the Sea," in London's Leicester Square on Thursday. Spacey, who wrote, directed, and starred in the film, paid tribute to Bobby Darin, a 1950s crooner he portrays in the movie. "This is my tribute to someone I think was a remarkable talent," Spacey said of Darin. Bosworth, 21, plays Darin's wife - real-life Hollywood actress Sandra Dee - in the film. "I knew absolutely nothing about Bobby Darin before this film, but now I'm a huge fan," said Bosworth, who attended the premiere with her boyfriend Orlando Bloom.

The CFTC has approved new NYMEX Rule 6.40C, which allows futures commission merchants to use their own internal, proprietary systems to generate average price ("AP") calculations when multiple prices are received on an order or series of orders. The rule is effective as of October 3, 2000.
The notice informs NYMEX and COMEX members that they should be aware of the new rule and its effective date. The rule allows FCMs to use their own internal, proprietary systems to calculate APs under certain conditions specified in the rule. This may provide greater flexibility for FCMs in their operations and may allow them to more efficiently calculate APs for their customers.

Another week has just flown by. I drove past the new house yesterday, and noticed the front window was covered in boxes. We may receive the keys at closing, and I'm getting so excited. If everything goes smoothly this week, we'll be ready to close a week from Wednesday. I was up until after 3:00 a.m. last night watching movies with Bethany; there's been a lot of movie-watching going on around here lately. Here's my rundown: last night we watched "Thirteen," which is a film every mother of a teenager should see. It was better than I expected. Then we watched "Gigli." Yes, it was just as bad as the media claimed, but we had to see for ourselves.

The Israeli club is turning its attention to Africa, with four African players, including Zimbabwean goalkeeper Energy Murambadoro, ready to play for Hapoel Bnei Sakhnin in the UEFA Cup. Bnei Sakhnin is making history as the first Arab team to compete in European competitions, facing English Premier League team Newcastle United in the opening round. Murambadoro, who gained recognition at the African Nations Cup finals in Tunisia, played a crucial role in Bnei Sakhnin’s 6-1 victory over Albania’s Partizani Tirana in the previous round. He recently moved to Israel following a brief period with South African club Hellenic. Last season, the club won the Israeli Cup final and it is based in Sakhnin, near Haifa. Bnei Sakhnin is dedicated to promoting peace and cooperation in Israel through its strong ethical framework.

Shah Rukh Khan's mantra of success  courage, unwavering determination, and an unrelenting vision. These are the hallmarks of the most accomplished individuals, whose unyielding spirits refuse to accept defeat. To them, seemingly insurmountable challenges are merely a canvas waiting to be conquered. Our next speaker, Bhakti Sharma, embodies this unshakeable resolve, as she navigates the vast expanse of long-distance swimming with ease. Ladies and gentlemen, please join me in welcoming the fearless Bhakti Sharma. (applause)

Bhakti Sharma  Imagine a two-and-a-half-year-old child, carefree and full of wonder, riding pillion on a moped behind her mother on a sweltering summer afternoon in Rajasthan, with no notion of the journey's destination. And just 20 minutes later, that same child finds herself fully immersed in the cool waters of a pool, as if she had always been meant to be there.

Causal inference from observational data is a primary goal of many data analyses in the health and social sciences. However, academic statistics has often been skeptical of data analyses with a causal objective. The introduction of the term "data science" presents a historic opportunity to redefine data analysis in a way that naturally accommodates causal inference from observational data. Like others before us, we categorize the scientific contributions of data science into three classes of tasks: description, prediction, and counterfactual prediction (which encompasses causal inference). An explicit classification of data science tasks is necessary to discuss the data, assumptions, and analytics required to successfully accomplish each task. We argue that a failure to adequately describe the role of subject-matter expert knowledge in data analysis is a source of widespread misconceptions about data science.

Observations of thermally driven transverse vibrations of a photonic crystal waveguide (PCW) are reported. The PCW consists of two parallel nanobeams with a 240 nm vacuum gap between the beams. Models are developed and validated for the transduction of beam motion to phase and amplitude modulation of a weak optical probe propagating in a guided mode of the PCW for probe frequencies both far from and near the dielectric band edge. Since our PCW has been designed for near-field atom trapping, this research provides a foundation for evaluating the potential detrimental effects of thermal motion on optical atomic traps near the surfaces of PCWs. Long-term goals are to achieve strong atom-mediated links between individual phonons of vibration and single photons propagating in the guided modes of the PCW, thereby enabling optomechanics at the quantum level with atoms, photons, and phonons.

At Casa Pescatore, my greatest joy has always been ensuring every guest feels as comfortable and welcome as they would in their own home. Yesterday, I believe I achieved that. The neighborhood gathered on Perry Avenue with an enthusiasm some might call overwhelming. At 4:30 PM, my dearest friend Eric and his son Nunu arrived, bearing a massive pan of baked ziti. The tables were meticulously arranged, laden with my homemade dishes, while laughter and chatter filled the air like joyous bells.  

Then, in a moment that warmed my heart, my closest friend from Florida walked through the door—a reunion I’d longed for. His presence alone was a gift, but the delightful surprise was his wife joining him. With her expecting a child, this is the nearest I’ll come to unclehood, and I embraced the role wholeheartedly. I showered her with endless trays of food and tiramisu, savoring every second of their company.

The kindness of strangers never ceases to amaze me. Today, I attempted to do laundry with my kids in tow and was struck by how quickly even the most mundane tasks can become an ordeal. I was also reminded of the remarkable generosity of complete strangers, who went out of their way to be kind without any ulterior motive. It's not like they were trying to get in my pants or anything, I had kid drool all over me and was wearing my "wash-day" outfit. And let's be real, I'm not sure I'll ever be bathing again, so there wasn't even an olfactory incentive there! Currently, I'm composing this post and simultaneously watching the Collected No Doubt videos. I'm doing both of these things from my desk in my room.

Interfacial spin-orbit coupling in Josephson junctions provides an intriguing means to combine anomalous Hall and Josephson physics in a single device. We theoretically investigate how the superposition of both effects affects the transport properties of superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator/superconductor junctions. The transverse momentum-dependent skew tunneling of Cooper pairs through the spin-active ferromagnetic insulator interface generates sizable transverse Hall supercurrents, which we refer to as anomalous Josephson Hall effect currents. We generalize the Furusaki-Tsukada formula, initially established to quantify usual (tunneling) Josephson current flows, to evaluate the transverse current components and demonstrate that their amplitudes are widely adjustable via the spin-orbit coupling strengths or the superconducting phase difference across the junction. As a clear spectroscopic fingerprint of Josephson junctions, well-localized subgap bound states form around the interface.

Hands down, my favorite first-person shooter of all time. Run-and-gun rambos need not apply. Recoil and ballistics are realistic. Single hits to the chest, neck, or head from rifle-caliber weapons almost always result in death unless it just grazes you. Rounds drop over distance, and short, controlled bursts are required if you wish to remain on target. There is no magical ammunition pool. If you reload halfway through your magazine all the time, you will have a pouch full of half-full mags. You can't run forever. If you sprint for a while, your aim will suffer as you gasp heavily until you have caught your breath. Rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or on a windowsill to steady your aim. Accuracy suffers when standing.

Natural events like volcanic eruptions release some of the carbon trapped in rocks, while human activities, such as burning fossil fuels, release much more than all the volcanoes on Earth combined. And there's nothing wrong with PCs - if I were a full-bore gamer with time to do such a thing, I'd certainly have a PC system for the wealth of available games. Though, except for a few unique products, that huge wall of games at CompUSA starts getting repetitive after a while - at first, I'm envious, then I start noticing that it's really just a lot of different flavors of the same soda... first-person shooters, mystery games, strategy games, simulations. There are so many variations of Age of Empires that it becomes comical. AOE - with orcs! AOE - with spaceships! AOE - with fuzzy wombats! It's like the endless movie trailers that begin with the line: 'In a world...'

Hands down, my all-time favorite first-person shooter. This game isn't for run-and-gun Rambo types. Recoil and ballistics are remarkably realistic, with single hits to the chest, neck, or head from rifle-caliber weapons almost always resulting in death, unless the shot merely grazes you. Rounds drop significantly over distance, and short, controlled bursts are necessary to stay on target. There's no infinite ammunition pool; if you constantly reload mid-magazine, you'll end up with a pouch full of partially used magazines. Endless sprinting isn't an option either, as prolonged running will leave you gasping for breath, severely impacting your aim until you've caught your breath. To steady your aim, rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or on a windowsill. Accuracy also suffers when standing, making it essential to adapt your shooting stance.

Andrea Levy, a previous Orange Prize winner, has garnered the Whitbread Novel of the Year award for her book "Small Island." This victory positions her as the leading contender for the overall prize, having surpassed Booker Prize recipient Alan Hollinghurst's "The Line of Beauty." On another note, Geraldine McCaughrean has triumphed in the children's fiction category for the third time with "Not the End of the World." Each category winner progresses to compete for the Whitbread Book of the Year title, which offers a £25,000 prize. A distinguished panel of judges, including Sir Trevor McDonald, actor Hugh Grant, and author Joanne Harris, will convene on January 25 to select the overall winner, with the announcement to follow that evening. According to bookmaker William Hill, Levy's novel, based in London, is favored to win with 6/4 odds.

If downstream, why isn't it being contracted by EWEB? 2. What volume is being sold each day? You mention the term DCQ and also refer to a 20% tolerance above or below the estimated quantity. A starving orphan begs for a second serving of gruel. A spinster withers away in her worn wedding dress. A stone-hearted miser is tormented by the Ghost of Christmas Past. Even more than a century after his passing, these memorable characters from the works of Charles Dickens remain instantly recognizable. His body of work is so distinctive that it inspired its own adjective. But what elements of Dickens's writing make it so extraordinary? His fiction is rich with suspense, featuring brooding settings, unexpected plot twists, and compelling mysteries. These characteristics kept his readers always yearning for more. Originally, his stories were published in serial form, with a few chapters released at a time in inexpensive literary journals, before later being compiled into books.

Certainly, here is the polished text:

"Dhyvl.nsf Dave, I would be pleased to draft the transaction agreement form; however, I have several questions. 

1. Regarding the delivery point and the use of interruptible transport on the Northwest Pipeline: Is the transport occurring upstream or downstream of the delivery point? If it is downstream, why isn’t EWEB handling the contracting? 

2. What volume is being sold daily? You refer to a DCQ and mention a 20% tolerance above or below the estimated quantity. Typically, a DCQ transaction does not allow for such tolerance. 

3. Is this transaction envisioned to be a requirements-based one? Is the price consistent for volumes exceeding the estimated quantity, within an additional 20%?

Because this is the aftermath—the first week back from practicum was a struggle, but only in the second week has the smoke truly begun to clear. Post-practicum trauma was a hidden epidemic, one only now coming to light. I was affected, but meeting with the course coordinator eventually allowed me to unburden myself, and in doing so, I’ve prevented another person from being stuck in such a pedagogically deficient practicum school.

Anti-spam laws bite the spammer hard: the net's self-declared spam king is seeking bankruptcy protection. Scott Richter, the man behind Optinrealbig.com and billions of junk mail messages, said lawsuits had forced the company into Chapter 11. Optinrealbig was fighting several legal battles, most notably against Microsoft, which is pushing for millions of dollars in damages. The company said filing for Chapter 11 would help it try to resolve its legal problems while still keeping trading. Listed as the third biggest spammer in the world by junk mail watchdog Spamhaus, Optinrealbig was sued in December 2003 for sending mail messages that violated anti-spam laws. The lawsuit was brought by Microsoft and New York Attorney General Eliot Spitzer, who alleged that Mr. Richter and his accomplices sent billions of spam messages through 514 compromised net addresses in 35 countries.

Palms sweaty, heart racing, stomach in knots. You can't cry for help. Not only is your throat too tight to breathe, but it'd be so embarrassing. No, you aren't being stalked by a monster—you're speaking in public, a fate some deem worse than death. See, when you're dead, you feel nothing; at a podium, you feel stage fright. But at some point we've all had to communicate in front of people, so you have to try and overcome it. To start, understand what stage fright is. Humans, social animals that we are, are wired to worry about reputation. Public speaking can threaten it. Before a speech, you fret, 'What if people think I'm awful and I'm an idiot?'

When I look in the mirror today, I see a justice and education scholar at Columbia University, a youth mentor, an activist, and a future New York State senator. (Cheers) I see all of that—and a man who spent a quarter of his life in state prison—six years, to be exact, beginning as a teenager on Rikers Island for an act that nearly took a man’s life. But what brought me from there to here wasn’t the punishment I endured as a teenager in adult prison or the brutality of our legal system. Rather, it was the transformative environment of a classroom that revealed possibilities I never imagined—for myself or for our justice system as a whole. Just weeks before my parole release, a counselor urged me to enroll in a new college course offered within the prison.

Nonlinear fluorescence emission has emerged as a powerful tool for achieving high-resolution optical imaging. In this study, we investigate the anomalous fluorescence saturation phenomenon in nitrogen-vacancy centers within diamond crystals. Our experiments revealed a pronounced fluorescence quenching effect under intense laser excitation, which significantly enhanced the nonlinear emission characteristics and altered the spatial frequency profile of the fluorescence images.  

To leverage this effect, we developed a differential excitation technique that effectively captures high spatial frequency components. Through precise modulation of the excitation laser power, we achieved a remarkable 1.6-fold improvement in spatial resolution compared to conventional confocal microscopy. Given the straightforward experimental implementation and minimal computational requirements, we anticipate this approach will find broad applications in enhancing spatial resolution for quantum sensing and biological labeling using solid-state defect centers.

The tails of diboson production at the LHC are sensitive to the interference between standard model and higher dimension operators, parameterizing the effects of heavy new physics. However, helicity selection rules for diboson scattering amplitudes impose an obstruction to the naïve interference contributions of dimension six operators, causing the total diboson rate correction's leading contribution to cancel. In this case, carefully measuring the azimuthal decay angles "resurrects" the interference, recouping sensitivity to the "non-interfering" operators. We explore these signatures in detail, and find that the EFT uncertainties associated with higher-dimensional operators are uniquely well-suppressed by the construction of an asymmetry variable which is only generated by these non-interfering operators, relegating the effects of higher-dimensional, interfering operators to the same status as statistical errors in this observable.

I am sorry I cannot show you my face because if I do, the bad guys will come for me. My journey started 14 years ago. I was a young reporter who had just graduated from college. Then I got a scoop—a fairly simple story about police officers taking bribes from street hawkers. As a young reporter, I thought I should approach it differently to maximize its impact, since everyone knew it was happening, yet nothing was being done to stop it. So I decided to go undercover as a seller. While selling, I documented solid evidence. The impact was great—fantastic, even. This is what many call immersion journalism or undercover journalism.

We had an outing yesterday with my co-employees at the bank. We went to Guintubdan, La Carolota. It was the first time for most of us. We trekked to the falls. It was a 30-minute or so walk. We were all catching our breaths. Whew! But we made it. The water was chilly, like ice. You can feel your body getting numb because of the coldness. We only stayed for less than an hour because we headed for the nearby waterfalls. Another 30 minutes of trekking. But the trip was worth it because the falls looked calming and the whole scene is indeed breathtaking. It's like you can hear yourself being one with nature. Soaking myself in the cold water was like communing with Mother Earth. The cold air and water were enough to make you feel lulled to sleep.

The government is facing growing anger from local ministers over plans to allocate whitehall cash to keep council tax bills low. According to Nick Raynsford, the local government minister, he has held "pretty frank discussions" with fellow ministers regarding the matter. The reallocation of £512m from central to local government budgets, as outlined in his pre-budget report on Thursday, is part of a £1bn package aimed at helping English local authorities keep next year's council tax rises below 5%, which is likely to be a key issue in the upcoming general election. Mr. Raynsford emphasized that nearly all central government departments have a vested interest in ensuring the smooth running of local authorities, which are responsible for delivering essential services without significant council tax increases.

Thankfully, I had brought a bag of eggs just in case, so Connor had a great time finding eggs in the park. Sunday morning, he woke up so excited that the Easter Bunny had come. There's nothing better than seeing the excitement on your child's face. After the egg hunt, we all went to church. While the Easter Bunny is fun, we make sure our kids know the true meaning of the holidays. This was Megan's first Easter—she looked adorable in her jean dress and stockings!

In a previous post, I expressed a certain level of understanding towards parents, perhaps even siding with them. However, I've had a change of heart. Parents often believe they understand us completely, even assuming they can discern our emotions just by looking at us. The truth is, they don't. They hardly know us at all. After a recent argument I had with Nicole about Kelli, my parents wanted me to explain what was bothering me. Honestly, it's frustrating. I don't always have to share my troubles with them. To give some context, Kelli and Nicole simply do not get along, and that's the issue at hand.

I have two questions for you. One: whose shoulders do you stand on? And two: what do you stand for? These are two questions that I always begin my poetry workshops with for students because, at times, poetry can seem like a dead art form for old white men who just seem like they were born to be old, like, you know, Benjamin Button or something. I ask my students these two questions and then share how I answer them, which is in these three sentences: I am the daughter of Black writers, who are descended from freedom fighters who broke their chains and changed the world. They call me. These are words I repeat in a mantra before every single poetry performance. In fact, I was doing it in the corner over there. I was making faces.

Creating a comprehensive water weather service is essential, yet achieving this vision requires collective advocacy for accountability—without which securing necessary funding remains unlikely.  

Reflecting on my journey, I recall my first TED talk eight years ago, fresh from graduate school. In those brief moments on stage, I unknowingly defined the questions that would shape the next decade of my work. Like many in their twenties, I was driven by idealism, convinced that technology alone could solve the world’s most pressing water challenges. I had much to learn.  

It was tempting to believe that the greatest obstacles in water quality stemmed from difficulties in detection—and that the solution lay in developing simpler, faster, and cheaper sensors. But I soon realized how mistaken I was.

Electric network frequency (ENF) fluctuations represent a valuable tool in multimedia forensics. An innovative approach for ENF estimation is presented, leveraging temporal windowing based on the filter-bank capon spectral estimator. A novel GoHberg-Semencul factorization of the model covariance matrix is employed due to the Toeplitz structure of the covariance matrix. Moreover, this approach utilizes a non-rectangular temporal window in the pre-spectral estimation stage, offering improved performance compared to traditional methods. Rapid matrix inversions are achieved through the use of Krylov matrices, resulting in faster and more accurate ENF estimation. The proposed approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods when a short time window of 1 second is applied to power recordings, and yields highly accurate results in speech recordings both in terms of time complexity and accuracy.

The spreadsheet below gives the implicit commodity based on the effective date of four different rates for the same rate schedule (Schedule DR). The rate schedule effective from August 2000 through September 2000 yields a much larger commodity charge because SDG&E customers will receive a lump sum reimbursement from SDG&E over those two months, resulting in lower electric bills. Assuming that the lump sum reimbursement is not included in the implicit energy component, the average from June 2000 through January 2001 is 6.2 cents, and the implicit commodity average from February 2001 through December 2001 is 7.3 cents. The average implicit commodity from June 2000 through December 2001 is 6.9 cents. For deliveries starting September 1, 2000, the average would still be 6.9 cents.

We are often viewed as the last resort for addressing failed social policies. We cannot choose who comes to us or dictate how long they remain. We are left to manage individuals for whom all other social safety nets have failed—those who have slipped through the cracks. Other systems can't handle them, so we must. Our task is to contain and control them. Over the years, as part of the prison system, our nation, and society, we have become adept at this. However, this is not an achievement to take pride in. Currently, we incarcerate more people per capita than any other nation in the world. Moreover, there are more Black men in prison today than there were enslaved in 1850.

We introduce a novel, Skyrme-like conserved current in the effective theory of pions and vector mesons, rooted in the concept of hidden local symmetry. The associated charge is equivalent to the skyrmion charge for any smooth configuration. Moreover, there exist singular configurations that can be identified as n_f=1 baryons, charged under the new symmetry. Under this identification, the vector mesons play the role of Chern-Simons vector fields living on the quantum Hall droplet that forms the n_f=1 baryon. We propose that this current is the correct effective expression for the baryon current at low energies. This proposal yields a unified picture for the two types of baryons, enabling them to continuously transform into one another in a natural manner. Furthermore, Chern-Simons dualities on the droplet can be interpreted as a consequence of Seiberg-like duality between gluons and vector mesons.

Deep learning has solved many problems beyond the reach of heuristic algorithms. It has also been successfully applied in wireless communications, even though current radio systems are well-understood and optimal algorithms exist for many tasks. While some gains have been achieved by learning individual parts of a receiver, a better approach is to jointly learn the entire receiver. However, this often results in a challenging nonlinear problem for which the optimal solution is impractical to implement. To address this, we propose a deep fully convolutional neural network, DeepRx, which executes the entire receiver pipeline—from frequency domain signal stream to uncoded bits—in a 5G-compliant manner. We enable accurate channel estimation by carefully constructing the convolutional neural network's input using both data and pilot symbols.

I recently had the opportunity to watch Troy with my family, but unfortunately, it failed to leave a lasting impression on me. Despite its promising premise, the film's execution fell short of expectations. The lavish set design, while visually stunning, seemed to overshadow the narrative, resulting in a disjointed tone and underdeveloped themes. The retelling of the classic story lacked the emotional resonance and depth that would have made it truly compelling. Furthermore, the character development was lacking, leaving the audience without a strong emotional investment in the characters' journeys. The score, too, was a disappointment, bearing an uncanny resemblance to the iconic soundtrack of Gladiator. In contrast to Gladiator, which masterfully wove its haunting score into a gripping narrative, Troy's music felt like a pale imitation, failing to elevate the film's emotional impact.

These are grim economic times, my dear colleagues, grim economic times indeed. And so, I would like to lift your spirits with one of the remarkable, yet little-known, commercial success stories of the past two decades. Comparable, in its own unique way, to the accomplishments of Microsoft or Google. And it has weathered the current recession with remarkable resilience. I refer, of course, to the organized crime industry.

Organized crime has been around for a long time, I know, and these words of caution are well-founded. But over the past two decades, it has experienced an unprecedented expansion, now accounting for roughly 15 percent of the world's GDP. I prefer to refer to it as the global shadow economy, or McMafia, for short.

So, what triggered this extraordinary growth in cross-border criminal activity?

Two domes, each embodying contrasting design philosophies. One consists of thousands of steel components, while the other is crafted from a single silk thread. One is synthetic, the other organic. One is imposed upon the environment, the other shapes it. One is designed for nature, the other is designed by nature. Michelangelo famously said that when he gazed upon raw marble, he perceived a figure yearning to be liberated, with the chisel as his sole instrument. However, living beings are not sculpted; they grow naturally. Within our smallest units of life—our cells—resides all the information needed for each cell to function and replicate. Tools come with consequences. Since the Industrial Revolution, the design world has been largely influenced by the demands of manufacturing and mass production.

Let me take you on the thrilling journey of the Rosetta spacecraft, a mission that has captivated my passion over the past two years. The objective: to escort and successfully land a probe on a comet. To appreciate the significance of this quest, it's crucial to delve into the origins of our solar system. Around four and a half billion years ago, a cloud of gas and dust existed. At the heart of this cloud, our Sun was born and ignited. Simultaneously, what we now recognize as planets, comets, and asteroids also began to form. The prevailing theory suggests that as Earth cooled after its initial formation, it was heavily bombarded by comets. These impacts likely delivered not only water but also complex organic materials to our planet, potentially sparking the emergence of life.

Mutation-based fuzzing begins with a starting set of seed inputs that don't cause crashes, known as a corpus, and generates new inputs through mutation. Often, a corpus may include thousands of similar inputs, which can result in redundant fuzzing efforts when all seeds are mutated exhaustively. To optimize this, fuzzing tools include distillation utilities, like afl-cmin, which help select the smallest subset of seeds capable of triggering the same range of instrumentation data points as the full set. It is a common belief that reducing the number and overall size of seeds enhances fuzzing efficiency, a theory which we examine systematically. We present findings from over 34 CPU-years of fuzzing across five different distillation methods to assess their effectiveness in identifying bugs in real-world software.

Cardinal criticises Iraq war: billions of pounds spent on conflict in Iraq and the Middle East should have been used to reduce poverty, Cardinal Cormac Murphy-O'Connor has said. The head of the Catholic Church in England and Wales made the comments on BBC Radio 4 and will reiterate his stance in his Christmas midnight mass. The cardinal used a Christmas message to denounce the war in Iraq as a "terrible" waste of money. He and the Archbishop of Canterbury have both spoken out about the war. Speaking on BBC Radio 4's Thought for the Day slot, he criticised the fact that "billions" have been spent on war, instead of being used to bring people "out of dire poverty, malnourishment and disease". The cardinal said 2005 should be the year for campaigning to "make history poverty".

In the primordial era, before the dawn of human civilization, the celestial realm was inhabited by four brothers who roamed the vast expanse of the divine domain. This world, comprised of lush fields, rolling plains, and lush gardens, was the domain of these four siblings, who shared a bond of brotherhood, yet remained ignorant of their paternal lineage. Among them, one brother, Deminan, stood apart from the others, his body scarred by painful scabs that seemed to hold the secrets of his mysterious past. One day, while the supreme spirit, Yaya, was absent from his gardens, Deminan and his brothers slipped into Yaya's abode, their curiosity getting the better of them. After feasting on the bounty of Yaya's hospitality, they stumbled upon a colossal gourd suspended from the ceiling, and as they endeavored to uncover its secrets, they inadvertently let it fall.

The orientational dynamics in the isotropic phase of a comb-shaped nematic polymer featuring mesogenic and functional side groups were investigated using Kerr effect and dielectric spectroscopy. For the first time, it was observed that, unlike low-molecular-weight mesogens, the relaxation of electric birefringence in a mesogenic polymer melt above the nematic-isotropic phase transition temperature can be described by a superposition of multiple exponential processes, with two of them being dominant. These primary relaxation mechanisms alternate within a temperature range of approximately fifty degrees. Additionally, dielectric spectroscopy revealed two distinct orientational relaxation processes: the first arising from the rotation of the side mesogenic groups, and the second linked to the motion of the main chain segments.

Citizens of the world, we face a global crisis of unprecedented scale. Please stand by for a message from the Secretary-General of the United Nations, António Guterres.  

The climate emergency is the defining crisis of our time. We are in a race against time, and we are losing. There is a growing tide of impatience, especially among young people, with global inaction. We need more ambition from all: governments, cities, businesses, investors, and people everywhere.  

So I’m pleased you are launching TED Countdown. Your influence and ideas can help accelerate momentum for a carbon-neutral world by 2050. That is the only way to avert the worst impacts of global heating. We have the tools, the science, and the resources. Let us now enter this race with political will and energy.

Warning over tsunami aid website: Net users are being told to avoid a scam website that claims to collect cash on behalf of tsunami victims. The site looks plausible because it uses an old version of the official Disasters Emergency Committee webpage. However, DEC has no connection with the fake site and says it has contacted the police about it. The site is just the latest in a long list of scams that try to cash in on the goodwill generated by the tsunami disaster. The link to the website is contained in a spam email that is currently circulating. The message's subject line reads "Urgent Tsunami Earthquake Appeal" and its text bears all the poor grammar and bad spelling that characterises many other phishing attempts.

Manchester's rock band, Doves, have reached a new milestone, claiming the top spot on the UK album charts with their latest release, "Some Cities." This achievement unseats the flamboyant American group, Scissor Sisters, at the summit. The album's success follows the band's single "Black and White Town," which had climbed to number six. In the singles chart, R&B sensation Nelly has captured the number one position with his hit "Over and Over," featuring a collaboration with Tim McGraw. New singles from Girls Aloud, Akon, and Kaiser Chiefs have also secured spots in the top ten, alongside tracks by Futureheads and Usher. Furthermore, the recent re-release of Elvis Presley's "(Marie's the Name) His Latest Flame" has achieved the third position, just ahead of Girls Aloud's "Wake Me Up." Hip-hop artist Akon's "Locked Up" holds the fifth spot, while the highly-anticipated band from Leeds, Kaiser Chiefs, have marked their second chart appearance with "Oh My God," coming in at number six.

U2, the Irish rock band, have been honored at France's biggest music awards, but were edged out by the British boy band Blue to win the top prize. U2 received a special achievement award at the NRJ Music Awards, but Blue took the international group award. Maroon 5 was named the best new international artist, and took the best international song title for "This Love". Over five million radio listeners voted in the awards. The international male and female prizes went to Usher and Avril Lavigne respectively. Collecting his band's award from model Naomi Campbell at the Cannes ceremony, U2 frontman Bono said in French  'I'm not from this country, but I'll make a little confession to you - it's at the Côte d'Azur I feel at home.'

An electron is usually considered to have only one type of kinetic energy, but could it have more, for its spin and charge, or by exciting other electrons? In one dimension (1D), the physics of interacting electrons is captured well at low energies by the Tomonaga-Luttinger-liquid (TLL) model, yet little has been observed experimentally beyond this linear regime. Here, we report on measurements of many-body modes in 1D gated wires using a tunnelling spectroscopy technique. We observe two separate Fermi seas at high energies, associated with spin and charge excitations, together with the emergence of three additional 1D 'replica' modes that strengthen with decreasing wire length. The effective interaction strength in the wires is varied by changing the amount of 1D inter-subband screening by over 45%.

Research indicates that high-speed internet access is significantly boosting online self-expression. According to a report by the UK think tank Demos, a substantial segment of broadband users in Britain, approximately one-quarter, routinely upload content and manage personal websites. The report highlights how an always-on, fast internet connection is transforming internet usage patterns. Currently, over five million UK households have broadband, and this number continues to rise swiftly. The Demos report analyzed the influence of broadband on internet habits, revealing that more than half of the broadband users access the web before breakfast. Remarkably, one in five confessed to browsing the internet in the middle of the night. Most notably, the report asserts that broadband is propelling individuals toward more active online engagement.

Electric network frequency (ENF) fluctuations offer a powerful tool for multimedia forensics. An innovative method for ENF estimation involves temporal windowing, utilizing the filter-bank Capon spectral estimator. This technique employs a Gohberg-Semencul factorization of the model covariance matrix, leveraging its Toeplitz structure. Notably, it introduces the use of a temporal window—other than the typical rectangular form—before the spectral estimation phase, marking a first in ENF analysis. Krylov matrices facilitate rapid matrix inversions for efficient implementation. When applied with a brief one-second time window in power recordings, this approach surpasses current state-of-the-art methods. In the context of speech recordings, it delivers exceptionally high accuracy and efficiency in terms of both time complexity and result precision.

**Subject:** Mailbox Storage Limit Notification  

Dear User,  

This is an automated notification to inform you that your mailbox is nearing its storage capacity. Your current mailbox size is **77,301 KB**, approaching the limit of **75,000 KB**.  

### **How to Check Mailbox Size:**  
1. Right-click your mailbox (Outlook Today).

   *(This can also be applied to individual folders.)*

- Permanently delete unnecessary emails (e.g., old **Sent Items**, **Journal Entries**).

- To disable journaling:

As of this week, Enrononline has just started offering US/Canadian dollar swaps online. As part of my review in determining whether a counterparty can transact these products online, I examine the financial trading agreement database to ensure that there are no restrictions indicated, review the master swap agreement, and I also examine any board resolutions we may have received when we executed the master agreement. What I am finding is that in the old days when we asked for resolutions evidencing the authority to enter into the master agreement/derivative transactions, the resolutions often did not allow the counterparties to enter into currency transactions. Since transacting these currency transactions with our energy customers is a new area of business for us, I want to ensure that we all remain mindful to check these old resolutions and not rely solely on the'restrictions' contained in Lotus notes.

Israeli club looks to Africa for talent, with four African players, including Zimbabwean goalkeeper Energy Murambadoro, set to play for Israeli club Hapoel Bnei Sakhnin in the UEFA Cup. As the first Arab side to compete in European competition, Bnei Sakhnin will face English Premiership side Newcastle United in the first round. Zimbabwe's national team goalkeeper, Murambadoro, who gained recognition at the African Nations Cup finals in Tunisia, contributed to Bnei Sakhnin's 6-1 victory over Albania's Partizani Tirana in the previous round. Following a brief stint with South African club Hellenic, Murambadoro recently relocated to Israel. Bnei Sakhnin, based in Sakhnin near Haifa, won the Israeli Cup final last season and is known for promoting peace and co-operation within Israel, with a strong ethos to match.

Doves soar to the top of the UK album chart, Manchester rock band Doves have debuted at number one with their new release, Some Cities. The trio displaces flamboyant US act Scissor Sisters at the top spot. The album follows the success of the single Black and White Town, which reached number six. R&B star Nelly has the new number one single with "Over and Over", which sees him team up with Tim McGraw. Girls Aloud, Akon, and Kaiser Chiefs all have new singles in the top ten, as do Futureheads and Usher. The latest Elvis Presley re-release, "(Marie's the Name)", his latest flame, entered the chart at number three, just one place ahead of Girls Aloud's "Wake Me Up". Hip-hop performer Akon's "Locked Up" is at number five, while hotly-tipped Leeds band Kaiser Chiefs have their second chart hit at number six with "Oh My God."

I have only had this game for a few months, but in just that short amount of time, two DLCs have come out, along with patches that have made an already great game into an excellent one. The devs are very active in the community, and it really shows in the various discussion forums and dev diaries. There is a fairly steep learning curve to the game's mechanics, which I am still trying to learn; however, this breadth of strategy allows you a myriad of ways to reach goals and take advantage of certain situations. The game, in my mind, is almost infinitely replayable. I find myself sucked into every session I play, regardless of how the play-through is going.

My stats (at the time of writing this review): 
- 8.6 hours to get to the end 
- 10 collectibles found out of 25 
- zero achievements out of 15 

What I liked: 
- Visuals up until the last level (ice caves) 
- Story is not that great, but the ending (after the closing credits) kind of made me not regret spending my time playing this game so much 

What I disliked: 
- Visuals in the last level are uninteresting 
- Gameplay in the last level gets much harder, making it a frustrating experience of having to do a lot of things over and over again until you get it right 
- Achievements are too hard; there are 13 (out of 15) that only less than 2.1% of players achieved.

A novel approach to mitigating the adverse effects of selfish routing on network efficiency in autonomous vehicle systems is presented, building upon the concept of routing controllability. However, the conventional assumption that autonomous vehicles would willingly submit to external control by a central authority is overly simplistic. This paper introduces a pioneering joint routing and pricing control framework, designed to motivate autonomous vehicles to adopt system-optimal routing strategies by offering toll savings, while simultaneously imposing congestion charges on user equilibrium-seeking autonomous vehicles and human-driven vehicles. This complex problem is formulated as a bi-level optimization paradigm, wherein dynamic tolls are optimized at the upper level, and a mixed equilibrium simulation-based dynamic traffic assignment model is employed at the lower level to account for the interactions between autonomous and human-driven vehicles in a mixed fleet environment.

The estimation of a dataset's intrinsic dimension is a crucial step in most dimensionality reduction techniques. This article introduces intrinsic, an R package that implements state-of-the-art likelihood-based estimators of a dataset's intrinsic dimension. Specifically, the package includes the two-nn, gride, and hidalgo models. To facilitate easy access to these novel estimators, the package provides high-level, intuitive functions built on a foundation of efficient, low-level routines. Intrinsic encompasses two categories of models: homogeneous and heterogeneous intrinsic dimension estimators. The homogeneous category comprises the two-nn and gride models, for which functions are available to perform inference under both frequentist and Bayesian frameworks. In contrast, the heterogeneous category features hidalgo, a Bayesian mixture model, which is accompanied by an efficient Gibbs sampler implementation.

I've recently embarked on a new gaming adventure, and I must confess that I'm utterly enthralled by this game. Hours can easily slip away as I become completely immersed in the experience, a phenomenon that hasn't occurred with a game in a while. The immersive atmosphere is largely due to the masterful combination of the soundtrack, sound effects, and controls, which all work in harmony to transport me to a world of endless excitement. I eagerly anticipate each combat encounter, and the developers have skillfully optimized the combat mechanics to ensure an exhilarating experience. Initially, I harbored some reservations about the naval combat system, but it's surprisingly enjoyable to unleash havoc on multiple fleets simultaneously. The only way to succumb to defeat is to neglect the importance of strategic retreats and regrouping, and instead, I've found myself getting outflanked and defeated. Kudos to the developers for crafting an experience that has truly left an indelible mark on my gaming journey.

Monday, December 11, 2000  

**To:** All PX Market Participants  
**From:** John Yurkanin, Chief Operating Officer  
**Re:** Loss of Continuity in PX Day-Ahead Auction Due to ISO Rule Changes  

The emergency tariff filing by the California ISO last Friday has adversely impacted the PX day-ahead auction market. The combination of soft and hard caps creates a complete disconnect, introducing unacceptable risk for all participants. Furthermore, it is unlikely to achieve the ISO’s intended reduction in real-time energy sales and procurement.  

The broader consequence will be that supply will continue to favor the higher realized price opportunities offered by the ISO. In response, the PX is petitioning Terry Winter of the ISO to promptly permit adjustment bids on any submitted schedule at any relevant price.

The paper "Imaging with High Spatial Resolution Using a Differential Excitation Protocol" by Xu et al. presents a novel method for imaging with high spatial resolution using a differential excitation protocol. The proposed method utilizes a differential excitation protocol to extract high spatial frequency information from the fluorescence emission of a sample. The authors demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method by imaging a phantom with a resolution of 1.6 times higher than confocal microscopy. They also show that the proposed method can be used for improving the spatial resolution of sensing and biological labeling with the defects in solids.

The paper begins with an introduction to the challenges of imaging with high spatial resolution, particularly in the context of biological imaging. The authors note that the resolution of conventional imaging techniques is limited by the diffraction of light and the finite size of the imaging system. They argue that a novel approach is needed to overcome these limitations and achieve high spatial resolution imaging.
The proposed method is based on the use of a differential excitation protocol, which modulates the excitation laser's power to extract high spatial frequency information from the fluorescence emission of a sample. The authors explain that the differential excitation protocol allows for the separation of the high spatial frequency information from the low spatial frequency information, which can be used to improve the spatial resolution of imaging. I recently completed the game, finishing it late last night. Although the story may not be particularly deep or captivating, the narrative shines through its engaging storytelling. The atmosphere is rich and immersive, with the many collectibles doing an excellent job of fleshing it out. I learned a lot about the world of Batman, which was a bit disappointing given the scarcity of boss battles. However, there are certainly more games in the future that will rectify this. The combat is moderately challenging, but leans towards the easier side. Leveling up Batman wasn't particularly satisfying, but the gadgets and upgrades you acquire during the campaign add a pleasant new layer of gameplay mechanics. Gaining access to previously inaccessible areas creates a sense of accomplishment and progression.

I'm sorry I cannot show you my face, because if I do, the bad guys will come for me. My journey started 14 years ago. I was a young reporter, having just graduated from college. Then I got a scoop - a relatively simple story. Police officers were taking bribes from hawkers selling on the streets. As a young reporter, I thought I should approach it differently to have maximum impact, since everyone knew it was happening, yet nothing was being done to stop it. So I decided to go undercover and act as a seller. As part of this, I was able to document hard-core evidence. The impact was great, fantastic. This is what many call immersion journalism, or undercover journalism.

While I personally cannot recommend this game, it wasn't absolutely terrible. The gunplay is alright, so long as you have the money to purchase gun parts, which would be my main complaint with this game: it's pay-to-win. If I could get past that, the game would be enjoyable, as the hardsuit is fun to use and HRV is an excellent idea. I'd take this game over any Call of Duty title any day, but its pay-to-win microtransaction format is off-putting for me. If you are alright with this model, then by all means, have fun. But if you're like me and dislike pay-to-win games, just let this one slide; it's not our cup of tea.

Baryonic feedback effects lead to a suppression of the weak lensing angular power spectrum on small scales. The poorly constrained shape and amplitude of this suppression are an important source of uncertainties for upcoming cosmological weak lensing surveys such as Euclid or LSST. In this first paper in a series of two, we use simulations to build a Euclid-like tomographic mock data-set for the cosmic shear power spectrum and the corresponding covariance matrix, which are both corrected for baryonic effects following the baryonification method of Schneider et al. (2019). In addition, we develop an emulator to obtain fast predictions of the baryonic power suppression, allowing us to perform a likelihood inference analysis for a standard $\Lambda$CDM cosmology with both cosmological and astrophysical parameters.

Entanglement is a key resource for quantum information processing. A widely used tool for detecting entanglement is entanglement witness, which measures the witness operator and is guaranteed to be positive for separable states and can be negative for entangled states. In reality, due to the exponentially increasing Hilbert space dimension with respect to the system size, it is very challenging to construct an efficient entanglement witness for general multipartite entangled states. For $n$-partite Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger (GHZ)-like states, the most robust witness scheme requires $n+1$ local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/2$ white noise. As a comparison, the most efficient witness for GHZ-like states only needs two local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/3$ white noise.

Causal inference from observational data is a central objective in many health and social science studies. Yet, academic statistics has traditionally viewed causal analyses with skepticism. The emergence of "data science" presents a pivotal opportunity to reshape data analysis in a way that inherently supports causal inference from observational data. Following earlier frameworks, we categorize the scientific contributions of data science into three core tasks: description, prediction, and counterfactual prediction (encompassing causal inference). A clear taxonomy of these tasks is essential to examine the data requirements, assumptions, and analytical methods needed for each. We contend that neglecting to articulate the role of domain expertise in data analysis has led to widespread misconceptions about data science.

This study presents a generalization of human gait analysis based on existing models from the literature, such as those by Keller and Kockshenev. The proposed hybrid model integrates a strategy for minimizing metabolic energy with a nonlinear approach to describing the center of mass's movement, aiming to simulate the walk-run transition. Experimental data across various speed regimes suggest that the trajectory's perimeter of the center of mass plays a significant role in quantifying this dynamic. The experimental procedure was conducted in partnership with the research group in biomedical engineering, basic sciences, and laboratories at Manuela Beltrán University in Bogotá, Colombia.

'to the moon' is no mere game... Nay, it is an unparalleled experience, and it ranks among the best of my life. Think The Walking Dead is sad? Hohoho! Well, it's no match for To the Moon! Never before have I been so deeply moved yet fulfilled over something purely fictional. This masterpiece represents the pinnacle of narrative craftsmanship in my opinion, and the soundtrack is a beautifully poignant and flawless accompaniment. Yes, the controls can be a tad finicky, and the gameplay may not be its strongest suit, but the story is so exceptionally crafted that I found myself utterly absorbed. Plus, the game's humor is delightful and tastefully woven into the narrative, never detracting from its dignified tone. The Doctor Who references are plentiful and delightful Easter eggs, adding an extra layer of charm to an already captivating experience.

We present a new mixed-variable symplectic (MVS) integrator for planetary systems that fully resolves close encounters. The method is based on a time regularisation that maintains the stability properties of symplectic integrators while reducing the effective step size during planetary encounters. We employ a high-order MVS scheme, enabling integration with large time steps far from close encounters. This algorithm can resolve near-exact collisions, with mutual separations of a fraction of the physical radius, using the same time-step as in weakly perturbed problems, such as the solar system. We demonstrate the long-term behaviour of the algorithm on systems of six super-Earths experiencing strong scattering over 50 kyr. Our results show that, for an equivalent computational cost, our algorithm achieves significantly better energy conservation compared to hybrid methods like Mercury.

The article proposes a new protocol for sequential measurements of a quantum system, which is based on the principles of stochastic dynamics and zeno physics. The protocol aims to confine and protect quantum dynamics from decoherence in accordance with the Zeno physics, by means of repeated measurements and dynamical decoupling control pulses. The protocol consists of three main tasks:
 (i) analytically predict the probability that a quantum system is transferred into a target quantum state by means of stochastic sequential measurements. (ii) analytically predict the probability that a quantum system is transferred into a target quantum state by means of stochastic sequential measurements. (iii) optimally reconstruct the spectral density of environmental noise sources by orthogonalizing in the frequency domain the filter functions driving the designed quantum-sensor.

The proposed protocol has the potential to enhance our capability to observe and manipulate open quantum systems, thus bringing advances to quantum science and technologies. The protocol can be used to study the dynamics of open quantum systems, which are quantum systems interacting with their environments, and to develop new quantum technologies such as quantum sensors and quantum communication.

The article is written in a clear and concise manner, with a good balance between theoretical background and experimental perspectives. The authors provide a detailed explanation of the Zeno physics and its relevance to the protocol

The proposed approach introduces a powerful tool in multimedia forensics, leveraging the frequency fluctuations of an electric network. An efficient method for estimating network frequency fluctuations is introduced, utilizing a novel approach based on temporal windowing and the filter-bank Capon spectral estimator. The proposed method employs a type of Goerg-Semencul factorization of the model covariance matrix, which is feasible due to the Toeplitz structure of the covariance matrix. Furthermore, this approach innovatively employs a non-rectangular temporal window, rather than a traditional rectangular one, prior to spectral estimation. Krylov matrices are utilized for the efficient implementation of matrix inversions. The proposed approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in network frequency estimation, particularly when a short time window of 1 second is employed in power recordings. In speech recordings, the proposed approach yields highly accurate results in terms of both time complexity and accuracy.

On Saturday, we took Connor to Fireman's Park, assuming the Easter egg hunt was scheduled for 11am. However, it turned out to be on Friday. Fortunately, I had brought a bag of eggs as a precaution, so Connor still had a great time searching for them in the park. On Sunday morning, he woke up thrilled to discover that the Easter bunny had visited. There's nothing quite like seeing the excitement on your child's face. Following the egg hunt, we attended church as a family. While the Easter bunny is a fun tradition, we make sure our kids understand the true meaning behind the holiday. This was Megan's first Easter, and she looked adorable in her jean dress and stockings.

Brizzel to Defend 200m Title at AAA Championships in Sheffield

Paul Brizzel, the talented Irish sprinter, will be among eight of Ireland's European Indoor hopefuls competing in this weekend's AAA Championships in Sheffield. With his impressive performance in the British Trials, Brizzel is eager to defend his 200m title. The in-form athlete has been dominating the European circuit in recent weeks, and his recent ranking as the fastest 800m runner in the world this winter has solidified his position as a top contender in the Madrid championships.

Meanwhile, US-based Alistair Cragg and Mark Carroll, the only Irish athletes selected so far for the Europeans, will not compete in Sheffield. Cragg, ranked number one in the world 3000m ranking, will be focusing on his event in Madrid, while Carroll, ranked number three in the world 3000m ranking, has confirmed his participation in the European championships.

In-form James McIlroy will hope to confirm his place in the British team for Madrid by winning the 800m title in Sheffield.
