Two identical domes represent two strikingly different design philosophies. One is constructed from thousands of steel components, while the other emerges from a single silk thread. One stands as a synthetic creation, and the other thrives organically. One imposes itself on its surroundings, whereas the other shapes its own environment. One is meticulously designed for nature, while the other is crafted by nature itself. Michelangelo famously observed that, within raw marble, he saw a figure striving to emerge. His chisel was his sole tool. In contrast, living organisms aren't sculpted; they grow and develop. Within each of our cells lies all the information necessary for every other cell's functioning and reproduction. It's important to remember that tools have profound impacts. Since the industrial revolution, design has been largely governed by the demands of manufacturing and mass production.

I'd like to take you on the epic quest of the Rosetta spacecraft. My passion for the past two years has been to escort and land the probe on a comet. In order to do that, I need to explain something to you about the origin of the solar system. When we go back four and a half billion years, there was a cloud of gas and dust. At the center of this cloud, our Sun formed and ignited. Alongside it, what we now know as planets, comets, and asteroids also formed. According to theory, once the Earth had cooled down a bit after its formation, comets massively impacted the planet and delivered water. They probably also brought complex organic material, which may have sparked the emergence of life.

My journey to becoming a polar specialist and photographer began at the age of four when my family relocated from southern Canada to northern Baffin Island, near Greenland. We settled in a small Inuit community of about 200 people, and we were one of only three non-Inuit families there. In this remote area, we had no television, computers, or even a radio. We didn't have a telephone either. I spent all my time outdoors with the Inuit, playing and learning from them. The snow and ice became my playground, and the Inuit were my mentors. This experience kindled my deep fascination with the polar regions.

World leaders convene amidst growing uncertainty as over 2,000 business and political figures from across the globe arrive at the Swiss mountain resort of Davos for the annual World Economic Forum (WEF). Over five days, they will deliberate on topics ranging from China's economic influence to Iraq's prospects following Sunday's elections. Prominent attendees include UK Prime Minister Tony Blair and South African President Thabo Mbeki, among more than 20 leaders and heads of state. In contrast to previous years, protests against the WEF are anticipated to be subdued. Anti-globalization activists have canceled a demonstration planned for the weekend. Meanwhile, the Brazilian city of Porto Alegre will host the alternative World Social Forum, scheduled to coincide with Davos's more lavish event.

Upon initial registration, a new user typically lacks a digital footprint on a website, characterized by an absence of interactions with items, a non-existent user profile, and no established social connections with other users. Despite this void of information, it is crucial for recommender systems to captivate these users from the outset, encouraging them to remain on the platform and evolve into active participants. This challenge falls under the category of new user cold-start, a critical issue that can significantly impact a company's growth and sustainability. Current approaches to addressing user cold-start recommendations often necessitate additional user engagement, such as participating in an interview process, or rely on supplementary information, including demographic data, geographical location, and social relationships. However, users may be reluctant to invest time in such processes, and supplementary information is frequently unavailable for cold-start users, rendering these approaches ineffective.

Metal ion implantation into ceramics has been demonstrated to be an effective and controllable technique for tailoring the surface electrical conductivity of ceramic pieces, and this approach has been used in numerous applications. Importantly, it provides a method for grading the voltage drop across high-voltage insulators, thereby increasing the maximum operational voltage that can be applied without surface flashover. However, a concern regarding this method is the long-term stability of the implantation-induced conductivity, particularly if the implanted metal species is readily oxidized. Here, we report on our examination of the long-term behavior of the surface conductivity of titanium-implanted alumina. The results indicate that after an initial drop of up to 40% in the first few weeks post-implantation, the conductivity exhibits only a very slow decrease of approximately 10% over the following year.

(Takes a deep breath) So, there was a time when music wasn't my primary source of income. For about five years after graduating from a reputable liberal arts university, I had a rather unique day job. (Chuckles) I was a self-employed living statue known as the Eight-Foot Bride. I love sharing this part of my life because people are always curious about who these so-called street performers are beyond the act. (Laughs) So, here I am. I would paint myself white, stand on a box, and place a hat or can at my feet. When someone dropped in money, I would give them a flower and engage in some intense eye contact. If they declined the flower, I would respond with a gesture of sadness and longing as they walked away.

Last night in class, we delved into that week's assigned reading, "Getting Back into Place: Toward a Renewed Study of the Place-World" by Edward S. Casey. The first chapter explores "implacement" and the second, "displacement." In the first chapter, Casey distinguishes that our contemporary world and culture are centered around time as the primary situating force, where phrases like "running out of time," "saving time," and "just in time" dominate our discourse. He argues that by placing place at the heart of culture, we can foster a more grounded community. (Please note that this is a rough summary, and I offer this disclaimer in case my professor or classmates happen to read this post.) So far, this book is the first reading that has truly resonated with me.

That's a rather obvious statement, isn't it? I began with that sentence about 12 years ago, initially in the context of developing countries, but you're here today from every corner of the world. Notably, those are often the places from which trouble originates. We have an ironic problem – good teachers tend to avoid the very places where they're needed the most. I started in 1999 to try and address this problem with a simple experiment in New Delhi.

Spontaneous conversations in real-world settings, such as those found in child-centered recordings, have proven to be among the most challenging audio files to process. However, developing speech processing models that can handle a wide range of conditions would be particularly valuable for language acquisition studies, where researchers are interested in the quantity and quality of speech that children hear and produce, as well as for early diagnosis and measuring the effects of remediation. In this paper, we present our approach to designing an open-source neural network that classifies audio segments into vocalizations produced by the child wearing the recording device, vocalizations produced by other children, adult male speech, and adult female speech. To achieve this, we compiled diverse child-centered corpora, totaling 260 hours of recordings and covering 10 languages.

Interfacial spin-orbit coupling in Josephson junctions presents an intriguing means of combining anomalous Hall and Josephson physics in a single device. We investigate theoretically how the superposition of both effects impacts the transport properties of superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator/superconductor junctions. Transverse momentum-dependent skew tunneling of Cooper pairs through the spin-active ferromagnetic insulator interface generates sizable transverse Hall supercurrents, which we refer to as anomalous Josephson Hall effect currents. We generalize the Furusaki-Tsukada formula, which was initially established to quantify usual (tunneling) Josephson current flows, to evaluate the transverse current components and demonstrate that their amplitudes are widely adjustable by means of the spin-orbit coupling strengths or the superconducting phase difference across the junction. As a clear spectroscopic fingerprint of Josephson junctions, well-localized subgap bound states form around the interface.

Weight pruning has been widely acknowledged as a straightforward and effective method for eliminating redundancy in deep neural networks (DNNs), thereby achieving acceleration on various platforms. However, most pruning techniques essentially involve trade-offs between model accuracy and regularity, leading to impaired inference accuracy and limited on-device acceleration performance. To address this issue, we introduce a new sparsity dimension, namely pattern-based sparsity, which comprises pattern and connectivity sparsity, and achieves both high accuracy and hardware friendliness. With carefully designed patterns, the proposed pruning method consistently and unprecedentedly achieves accuracy enhancement and better feature extraction ability across different DNN structures and datasets. Our pattern-aware pruning framework also enables simultaneous pattern library extraction, pattern selection, pattern and connectivity pruning, and weight training. Our approach, based on the new pattern-based sparsity, naturally fits into compiler optimization for highly efficient DNN execution on mobile platforms.

Over the weekend, Governor Davis and Senator Polanco reached an informal agreement in which Senator Polanco agreed to sponsor the SoCal MOU bill. The bill is anticipated to be introduced during the new extraordinary session, starting today or tomorrow. Initially, it will be reviewed by the Senate Energy Committee. However, the committee's chair, Senator Bowen, is staunchly opposed to the MOU. Consequently, the bill is expected to be tied up in committee hearings for a significant period. Yesterday, my colleagues from the bank and I embarked on an adventure to Guintubdan, La Carolota. For most of us, it was a brand new experience. We hiked towards the waterfalls, a journey that took approximately 30 minutes. It left us all a bit breathless, but we persevered and reached our destination. The water was incredibly cold, like ice, numbing our bodies as we dipped in. We spent less than an hour there before heading to another set of nearby falls, requiring another 30-minute trek. The effort was undoubtedly worthwhile, as the falls exuded a tranquil beauty, and the entire scene was breathtaking. It felt as though one could truly connect with nature, becoming part of its serene rhythm. Immersing myself in the frigid water was akin to communing with Mother Earth, while the crisp air and chilled water could easily lull you into a peaceful slumber.

More women are taking charge of protecting their home computers against malicious net attacks, according to a two-year study. The number of women buying programs to protect PCs from viruses, spam, and spyware rose by 11.2% each year between 2002 and 2004, the study found. Security messages are reaching a diverse range of surfers, with 40% of those buying home net security programs being retired. In the last three years, this number has increased by an average of 13.2%. Additionally, more retired women (53%) are buying security software than retired men. This reflects the changing stereotype and demographics of web users, as well as growing awareness of the greater risks that high-speed broadband net connections can pose to surfers.

You've been accused of a crime you did not commit. It's impossible to prove your innocence. If you insist on maintaining your innocence, you'll likely be found guilty and executed. However, if you confess, apologize, and implicate others for good measure, you'll go free. This was the choice facing those accused of witchcraft in the village of Salem, Massachusetts between February 1692 and May 1693. They were the victims of paranoia about the supernatural, misdirected religious fervor— and a justice system that valued repentance over truth. Salem was settled in 1626 by Puritans, a group of English Protestants. Life was strict and isolated for the people of Salem. Battles with their Native American neighbors and groups of French settlers were commonplace.

I have two questions for you. One: whose shoulders do you stand on? And two: what do you stand for? These are two questions I always begin my poetry workshops with students, because at times, poetry can seem like a dead art form for old white men who appear to have been born old, like Benjamin Button. I ask my students these two questions, and then I share how I answer them, which is in these three sentences: I am the daughter of black writers, who are descended from freedom fighters who broke their chains and changed the world. They call me. These are words I repeat in a mantra before every single poetry performance. In fact, I was doing it in the corner over there, making faces.

One of the most pressing unsolved challenges in condensed matter physics is understanding how two-dimensional systems can exhibit unconventional superconductivity at high critical temperatures. Ultracold Fermi gases have emerged as ideal model systems to investigate the intricate interplay between strong correlations and reduced dimensionality, yet direct evidence of superfluidity in these systems has been lacking. Here, we provide conclusive evidence of superfluidity in an ultracold Fermi gas by inducing a periodic potential and observing the absence of dissipation below a critical velocity, v_c. Our measurement of v_c as a function of interaction strength reveals a maximum in the crossover regime between bosonic and fermionic superfluidity. This achievement establishes ultracold Fermi gases as a powerful platform for exploring the impact of reduced dimensionality on strongly correlated superfluids and sheds new light on the fundamental physics governing these systems.

**Polished Version:**  

Though fate denied us the bonds of marriage or children, though we were never bound by vows or titles, a part of you will forever dwell within me. My love for you was boundless, and its echo lingers still—a quiet, persistent whisper in my heart.  

I will always cherish those fleeting nights when we were one, our bodies and souls intertwined beneath the vast, starlit sky by the shore, or the memory of your lips meeting mine in the rain. Even now, when sorrow pierces me like shards of dark glass, you are there—that fragment of you, alive within me, bleeding the love that once burned so fiercely… and never truly faded.  

For her.

The film's lead actors, Paul Giamatti, Thomas Haden Church, and Virginia Madsen, also received acting accolades. "Sideways" is anticipated to perform well at Sunday's Oscars, where it has garnered five nominations. Celebrating its 20th anniversary, the Spirit Awards spotlight films produced outside the conventional studio framework and traditionally occur the day prior to the Oscars. Among other notable winners, Catalina Sandino Moreno received the Best Actress award for her portrayal of a drug smuggler in the Colombian drama "Maria Full of Grace." Moreno is also nominated for Best Actress at the Oscars. The Best First Screenplay award was conferred to Joshua Marston for "Maria Full of Grace.

In our research, we explore the transport properties and superconducting proximity effect in NSN junctions that arise when a Weyl semimetal with broken time-reversal symmetry is in proximity to an s-wave superconductor. Our findings reveal that both the differential conductance and the induced pairing amplitudes are significantly influenced by the angle between the direction of the junction in real space and the axis separating the Weyl nodes in momentum space. We also highlight the impact of a chiral chemical potential, which refers to the electron population imbalance between Weyl nodes of opposite chirality, on the junction's transport features. Interestingly, we detect a net spin polarization of Cooper pairs generated via Andreev reflection within the two Weyl semimetal regions. This spin polarization is opposite in the two regions and is highly sensitive to variations in chirality imbalance and excitation energy.

A significant shift is underway in the realm of home computer security, with a growing number of women and older adults taking proactive steps to safeguard their systems against malicious online threats. According to a comprehensive two-year study conducted by Preventon, a leading net security firm, there has been a notable increase in the number of women purchasing software to protect their PCs from viruses, spam, and spyware. Between 2002 and 2004, this demographic saw an average annual growth rate of 11.2% in security software purchases. The study's findings also reveal that a substantial proportion of home net security program buyers are retirees, with this segment experiencing an average annual growth rate of 13.2% over the past three years. Notably, retired women are leading the charge, with 53% of this demographic purchasing security software, outpacing their male counterparts. These trends reflect a broader evolution in the demographics and online behaviors of web users, as well as a growing awareness of the heightened risks associated with high-speed broadband internet connections.

On October 4, 1957, the world watched in awe and fear as the Soviet Union launched Sputnik, the world's first man-made satellite, into space. This little metal ball, smaller than two feet in diameter, launched a space race between the U.S. and U.S.S.R. that would last for eighteen years and change the world as we know it. Sputnik was actually not the first piece of human technology to enter space. That superlative goes to the V-2 rocket used by Germany in missile attacks against Allied cities as a last-ditch effort in the final years of World War II. It wasn't very effective, but, at the end of the war, both the U.S. and U.S.S.R. had captured the technology and the scientists that had developed it and began using them for their own projects.

The tails of diboson production at the LHC exhibit sensitivity to interference effects between Standard Model processes and higher-dimensional operators that parameterize heavy new physics. However, helicity selection rules governing diboson scattering amplitudes create an obstruction to the naive interference contributions from dimension-six operators, leading to a cancellation of the leading-order correction to the total diboson production rate. Remarkably, precise measurements of azimuthal decay angles can "resurrect" this interference, thereby restoring sensitivity to these "non-interfering" operators. We conduct a detailed investigation of these signatures and demonstrate that the effective field theory (EFT) uncertainties related to higher-dimensional operators are exceptionally well-suppressed through the construction of an asymmetry variable. This observable is uniquely generated by the non-interfering operators, effectively reducing the impact of higher-dimensional, interfering operators to the level of statistical uncertainties in this measurement.

Here, we report the observation of thermally driven transverse vibrations in a photonic crystal waveguide, comprising two parallel nanobeams separated by a 240 nm vacuum gap. Theoretical models are developed and validated to describe the transduction of beam motion into phase and amplitude modulation of a weak optical probe propagating in a guided mode of the waveguide for probe frequencies both near and far from the dielectric band edge. This research provides a foundation for evaluating the potential detrimental effects of thermal motion on optical atomic traps near the surfaces of photonic crystal waveguides, which have been designed for near-field atom trapping. Ultimately, our long-term goal is to establish strong atom-mediated links between individual phonons of vibration and single photons propagating in the guided modes of the waveguide, thereby enabling optomechanics at the quantum level, harnessing the synergy between atoms, photons, and phonons.

The estimation of the intrinsic dimension of a dataset is a fundamental step in most dimensionality reduction techniques. This article illustrates Intrinsic, an R package that implements novel state-of-the-art likelihood-based estimators of the intrinsic dimension of a dataset. In detail, the methods included in this package are the two-nn, gride, and Hidalgo models. To allow these novel estimators to be easily accessible, the package contains a few high-level, intuitive functions that rely on a broader set of efficient, low-level routines. Intrinsic encompasses models that fall into two categories: homogeneous and heterogeneous intrinsic dimension estimators. The first category contains the two-nn and gride models. The functions dedicated to these two methods carry out inference under both the frequentist and Bayesian frameworks. In the second category, we find Hidalgo, a Bayesian mixture model, for which an efficient Gibbs sampler is implemented.

We introduce a systematic framework for decomposing the values of global Mackey functors associated with orthogonal, unitary, and symplectic groups. Notably, the restriction homomorphisms between these groups of consecutive dimensions admit natural splittings, yielding epimorphisms that are inherently split. This decomposition is of particular interest in the context of equivariant stable homotopy theory, where the equivariant stable homotopy groups of any global spectrum collectively form a global Mackey functor. Consequently, our splittings enable the disentanglement of intricate long exact homotopy group sequences into more manageable short exact sequences. Furthermore, when applied to the real and complex global Thom spectra, namely $\mathbf{mo}$ and $\mathbf{mu}$, these splittings reveal the regularity of various Euler classes that are intimately connected to the tautological representations of the orthogonal and unitary groups $o(n)$ and $u(n)$, respectively.

Khyatt (non-privileged).pst El Paso tests market for mainline expansion. El Paso Natural Gas apparently received the message when its last open season for 1.2 Bcf/d of firm transportation capacity attracted bids for 14.4 Bcf/d. The pipeline has announced an open season to test market interest in a mainline expansion and to address regulatory concerns that something needs to be done to alleviate the apparent capacity constraint at the California border. El Paso has been under significant pressure from regulators and shippers to expand due to enormous demand and skyrocketing prices in California. In a Jan. 3 letter to the pipeline, Daniel M. Adamson, Director of FERC's Office of Energy Projects, urged El Paso to consider modifying its Line 2000 project to "assist the difficult situation" now facing the California gas market.

Ranking algorithms play a crucial role in online platforms ranging from search engines to recommender systems. In this paper, we identify a surprising consequence of popularity-based rankings: the fewer the items reporting a given signal, the higher the share of overall traffic they collectively attract. This few-get-richer effect emerges in settings where there are few distinct classes of items (e.g., left-leaning news sources versus right-leaning news sources), and items are ranked based on their popularity. We demonstrate analytically that the few-get-richer effect emerges when people tend to click on top-ranked items and have heterogeneous preferences for the classes of items. Using simulations, we analyze how the strength of the effect changes with assumptions about the setting and human behavior. We also test our predictions experimentally in an online experiment with human participants. Our findings have important implications for understanding the spread of misinformation.

Debra Hill, the screenwriter and producer best known for her work on the 1970s horror classic Halloween, has died in Los Angeles at the age of 54. Hill, who had been suffering from cancer, co-wrote the 1978 film, which starred Jamie Lee Curtis as a babysitter terrorized by a psychopath. Directed by John Carpenter, it made over $60m (£31.3m) worldwide, a record for independent film at that time. Hill also collaborated with Carpenter on Escape from New York and The Fog. Born in New Jersey, Hill began her career as a production assistant and worked her way up the ranks, becoming an assistant director and second-unit director before she started collaborating with Carpenter. She was widely regarded as a pioneering woman in film, taking on jobs in the 1970s that were more commonly held by men.

Tories outlining policing plans: local communities would be asked to go to the polls to elect their own area police commissioner, under plans unveiled by the Conservatives. Party leader Michael Howard said the new role would replace 'inconspicuous' police authorities. He said the new office would not supersede the job of a chief constable. The Lib Dems said the plan could let extreme groups run policing, while Labour criticised 'extravagant' Tory promises on policing. Responding to the plans, the chairman of the Police Federation of England and Wales, which represents rank-and-file officers, said it was essential operational independence was retained. Jan Berry said: 'It is a service, not a political football to be kicked around every time an election approaches. These plans could result in those with extreme political views dictating what actually happens on the ground,' she warned.

The estimation of the intrinsic dimension of a dataset is a fundamental step in most dimensionality reduction techniques. This article illustrates intrinsic, an R package that implements novel state-of-the-art likelihood-based estimators of the intrinsic dimension of a dataset. In detail, the methods included in this package are the two-NN, Gride, and Hidalgo models. To allow these novel estimators to be easily accessible, the package contains a few high-level, intuitive functions that rely on a broader set of efficient, low-level routines. Intrinsic encompasses models that fall into two categories: homogeneous and heterogeneous intrinsic dimension estimators. The first category contains the two-NN and Gride models. The functions dedicated to these two methods carry out inference under both the frequentist and Bayesian frameworks. In the second category, we find Hidalgo, a Bayesian mixture model, for which an efficient Gibbs sampler is implemented.

Doves soar to the top of the UK album chart with their latest release, securing the number one spot, surpassing flamboyant US act Scissor Sisters. This marks a significant milestone for the trio, following the success of their single 'Black and White Town', which peaked at number six. Meanwhile, R&B sensation Nelly has taken the top spot with his new single 'Over and Over', a collaboration with Tim McGraw. The top ten is filled with a diverse range of artists, including Girls Aloud, Akon, Kaiser Chiefs, Futureheads, and Usher. The latest Elvis Presley re-release, '(Marie's the Name)', has debuted at number three, narrowly missing the top spot by one place. 'Wake Me Up' by Girls Aloud holds onto the number four position, while Akon's 'Locked Up' claims the number five spot. Hotly-tipped Leeds band Kaiser Chiefs have secured their second chart hit with 'Oh My God', which has landed at number six.

An electron is typically considered to possess only one type of kinetic energy, but could it have more, due to its spin and charge, or by exciting other electrons? In one dimension (1D), the physics of interacting electrons is captured well at low energies by the Tomonaga-Luttinger-liquid (TLL) model, yet little has been observed experimentally beyond this linear regime. Here, we report on measurements of many-body modes in 1D gated-wires using a tunnelling spectroscopy technique. We observe two separate Fermi seas at high energies, associated with spin and charge excitations, together with the emergence of three additional 1D'replica' modes that strengthen with decreasing wire length. The effective interaction strength in the wires is varied by changing the amount of 1D inter-subband screening by over 45%.

When constructing models to summarize clinical data for simulations, it is advisable to assess the models' capacity to replicate the data. This can be achieved by performing visual predictive checks (VPCs), which involve (1) several replications of the original study by simulation from the model under evaluation, (2) estimating values of interest for each simulated study and (3) comparing the distribution of those estimates with the estimate from the original study. This is a straightforward method that can be generally applied, particularly when considering the application to time-to-event data and taking into account the special case when a time-varying covariate is not known or cannot be approximated after event time.

In a bold move, the China Three Gorges Project Corp is reportedly flouting a government directive to suspend construction on one of its massive dams, according to Chinese state media. Despite being ordered to halt work, the company behind the iconic Three Gorges Dam is pressing ahead with the development of its sister project, the Xiluodu Dam. This defiance comes as the Xiluodu Dam is one of 30 large-scale construction projects that have been instructed to cease operations due to inadequate environmental assessments. Rather than comply, the company may opt to pay a penalty, as reported by the Beijing News. Furthermore, the China Three Gorges Project Corp has also disregarded shutdown orders at two of its other sites: the Three Gorges underground power plant and the Three Gorges project electrical power supply plant. To date, only 22 of the 30 construction projects targeted by China's State Environmental Protection Agency (SEPA) for failing to conduct mandatory environmental impact assessments have adhered to the agency's shutdown directive.

An exceptional AAA first-person shooter—this sums it up perfectly. The game excels in every aspect: stunning visuals, immersive audio, and flawless gameplay mechanics. It delivers a compelling narrative, memorable characters, impeccable voice acting, and an electrifying soundtrack. However, be warned—it's not for the sensitive, as certain cutscenes depict intense and graphic violence.  

That said, when a game reaches such high standards, its shortcomings become more noticeable. Here are the issues I encountered:

This game was a delightful surprise. I often come across rave reviews of certain products that are often misleading. In this case, the reviews reflect the fun of this charming little game. It's an intriguing blend between a classic 2D platformer and a MOBA, and it pulls it off with style. I found myself enjoying characters that I wouldn't typically enjoy in other MOBA games. I was pleasantly surprised with the outcome and ended up trying out more as they were unlocked from playing games. The art style and interface is quaintly charming. As such, I now use one of the characters as my avatar and a wallpaper from creating a card set for the game.

Mutation-based fuzzing generally begins with an initial set of valid seed inputs (a corpus) that are mutated to generate new test cases. Typically, such corpora contain numerous similar inputs, which can result in redundant fuzzing efforts when exhaustively mutating all available seeds. To mitigate this issue, modern fuzzers incorporate distillation tools (such as afl-cmin) that identify the minimal subset of seeds capable of exercising the same spectrum of code coverage as the complete corpus. Industry experience indicates that reducing both the quantity and total size of seeds enhances fuzzing efficiency—a hypothesis we methodically investigate. Our study presents findings from over 34 CPU-years of fuzzing experiments using five distinct distillation techniques, evaluating their effectiveness in discovering vulnerabilities in production software.

My mom has always been a bit of a puzzle-solver, and she's been trying to figure out my little brothers' birthdays for years. The Case of the Missing Birth Certificates It was a typical day in our household when my mom asked, "What day should we put for their birthday?" The direpack package aims to provide a set of modern statistical dimension reduction techniques as a single, consistent package in Python. The included methods fall into three categories: projection pursuit-based dimension reduction, sufficient dimension reduction, and robust regression estimators for dimension reduction. Additionally, the package offers regularized regression estimators based on these reduced dimension spaces, ranging from principal component regression to sparse partial least squares regression. Direpack also includes a variety of pre-processing utilities, such as generalized spatial signs, as well as dedicated plotting and cross-validation tools. Furthermore, the package is designed to be consistent with the scikit-learn API, allowing for seamless integration with other machine learning tools.

Urllink, let's give a big hello to the newest Atom/RDF/RSS reader on the block: Urllink PulpFiction 1.0 from Urllink Freshly Squeezed Software. It appears to come with a lot of native app goodness. It's worth noting that it is neither free nor open-source, which seems to be a 'hot topic' on the internet again. You can try it for 15 days and then switch to something else, or pay $25 if you like it. A feature that stands out to me is how each post is its own entity, which can be moved around, stored, labeled, and even deleted. I can easily see how this would make some news tracker enthusiasts very happy. It imports and exports OPML, as you might expect.

Continuous-time quantum walks have proven to be an extremely useful framework for designing several quantum algorithms. Often, the running time of quantum algorithms in this framework is characterized by the quantum hitting time: the time required for the quantum walk to find a vertex of interest with high probability. In this article, we provide improved upper bounds for the quantum hitting time that can be applied to several CTQW-based quantum algorithms. In particular, we apply our techniques to the glued-trees problem, improving its hitting time upper bound by a polynomial factor: from $o(n^5)$ to $o(n^2\log n)$. Furthermore, our methods also help exponentially improve the precision dependence of the continuous-time quantum walk based algorithm to find a marked node on any ergodic, reversible Markov chain by Chakraborty et al.

Ranking algorithms are pivotal in online platforms, spanning from search engines to recommender systems. This paper reveals a surprising consequence of popularity-based rankings: when fewer items convey a specific signal, they collectively attract a disproportionately higher share of overall traffic. This 'few-get-richer' phenomenon arises in settings with limited distinct classes of items, such as left-leaning versus right-leaning news sources, where items are ranked by popularity. We analytically demonstrate that this effect emerges when users tend to click on top-ranked items and exhibit heterogeneous preferences for item classes. Through simulations, we examine how the effect's strength varies with different assumptions about the setting and human behavior. We also experimentally validate our predictions in an online study with human participants, yielding findings with significant implications for understanding the dissemination of misinformation.

Where do I begin with this incredibly beautiful game? In this paper, we investigate multi-agent discrete-event systems with partial observation. The agents can be categorized into several groups, each comprising agents with isomorphic state transition structures, and can be relabeled into a single template. Based on this template, we design a scalable supervisor whose state size and computational cost are independent of the number of agents. This scalable supervisor, under partial observation, does not require recomputation regardless of how many agents are added or removed from the system. We generalize our earlier findings on partial observation by proposing sufficient conditions for the safety and maximal permissiveness of the least restrictive supervisor on the template level. An illustrative example is provided to demonstrate the proposed scalable supervisory synthesis.

I know this may seem unusual, but I believe robots can inspire us to become better humans. Growing up in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania, the birthplace of Bethlehem Steel, I had a unique upbringing. My father, an engineer, would teach me about the inner workings of things. We would collaborate on projects, such as model rockets and slot cars. Here's a photo of the go-kart we built together. That's me behind the wheel, accompanied by my sister and my best friend at the time. And one day, when I was around 10 years old, he came home, and at the dinner table, he announced that for our next project, we were going to build a robot, a robot, a machine that would learn and adapt to its environment, a true marvel of engineering and innovation.

Last night in class, we were discussing that week's assigned reading, "Getting Back into Place: Toward a Renewed Study of the Place-World," by Edward S. Casey. The first chapter deals with "implacement" and the second, "displacement." In the first chapter, Casey makes the distinction that our contemporary world and culture revolves around time as the situating force: we are running out of time, we have saved time, we are just in time. He is trying to make the case that using place as the center of culture gives a more grounded community. (That is a very rough summation, a disclaimer just in case my professor or any other classmate happens to read this post.) So far, this book is the first reading that has grabbed me by the proverbial shoulders.

According to Chinese state media, the China Three Gorges Project Corporation is defying a government directive to halt the construction of one of its massive dams. Despite orders, the company responsible for building the Three Gorges Dam is proceeding with the construction of the Xiluodu Dam, as reported by the Beijing News. The Xiluodu Dam is among 30 large-scale projects ordered to stop due to insufficient environmental assessments. Instead of complying, the company may choose to pay fines, as suggested by the Beijing News. Additionally, the firm has disregarded instructions to cease work on two other projects: the Three Gorges Underground Power Plant and the Three Gorges Project Electrical Power Supply Plant. To date, only 22 out of the 30 projects targeted by China's State Environmental Protection Agency (SEPA) for failing to perform mandatory environmental impact assessments have adhered to the suspension order.

A single vitamin has the potential to lower your risk of heart disease. Eating chocolate can relieve stress for students. A new medication extends the lives of patients with rare diseases. Health headlines like these are published daily and often present conflicting claims. There's frequently a gap between the sensationalism of headlines and the detailed, gradual findings of the medical research they reference. So, how can you avoid being deceived by such eye-catching headlines? The best approach is to examine the original research behind the headlines. We've created a hypothetical research scenario for each of these three headlines. Continue reading for an explanation of the first example; pause at the headline to consider the question. These are simplified scenarios.

I've only owned this game for a few months, yet in that brief period, two DLCs have already been released alongside patches that transformed an already outstanding game into something truly exceptional. Cardinal criticises Iraq war, saying billions of pounds spent on conflict in Iraq and the Middle East should have been used to reduce poverty. Cardinal Cormac Murphy-O'Connor made the comments on BBC Radio 4 and will reiterate his stance in his Christmas midnight mass. The head of the Catholic Church in England and Wales used a Christmas message to denounce the war in Iraq as a "terrible" waste of money. He and the Archbishop of Canterbury have both spoken out about the war. Speaking on BBC Radio 4's Thought for the Day slot, he criticised the fact that "billions" have been spent on war, instead of being used to bring people "out of dire poverty, malnourishment and disease". The cardinal said 2005 should be the year for campaigning to "make history poverty".

Even nature's most repulsive creatures possess vital secrets, yet who would welcome a swarm of cockroaches approaching? However, one of the fundamental differences between natural and human technologies lies in resilience. Robust systems remain stable in unfamiliar and challenging environments. Remarkably, cockroaches can self-stabilize while navigating uneven terrain with ease. When we integrated jet packs or introduced perturbations like earthquakes, we discovered that their exquisitely tuned legs allowed them to self-stabilize without diverting any mental resources. They can effortlessly traverse complex terrain, such as grass, without becoming unbalanced. In fact, we observed a novel behavior where, due to their shape, they automatically roll to their side to navigate through artificial test sections of grass. Robust systems can perform multiple tasks with the same underlying structure. In other words, here's a new behavior we've recently discovered.

Interfacial spin-orbit coupling in Josephson junctions offers an intriguing way to combine anomalous Hall and Josephson physics in a single device. We study theoretically how the superposition of both effects impacts the transport properties of superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator/superconductor junctions. Transverse momentum-dependent skew tunneling of Cooper pairs through the spin-active ferromagnetic insulator interface creates sizable transverse Hall supercurrents, which we refer to as anomalous Josephson Hall effect currents. We generalize the Furusaki-Tsukada formula, initially established to quantify usual tunneling Josephson current flows, to evaluate the transverse current components and demonstrate that their amplitudes are widely adjustable by means of the spin-orbit coupling strengths or the superconducting phase difference across the junction. As a clear spectroscopic fingerprint of Josephson junctions, well-localized subgap bound states form around the interface.

Ranking algorithms play a crucial role in online platforms, encompassing search engines and recommender systems. In this study, we uncover a counterintuitive consequence of popularity-based rankings  the fewer the items reporting a given signal, the more the collective traffic they collectively attract. This few-get-richer phenomenon arises in settings where there are limited distinct classes of items (e.g., left-leaning news sources versus right-leaning news sources), and items are ranked based on their popularity. We demonstrate analytically that this effect emerges when individuals tend to click on top-ranked items and exhibit heterogeneous preferences for the classes of items. Through simulations, we examine how the strength of the effect varies with assumptions about the setting and human behavior. We also conduct an online experiment with human participants to test our predictions. Our findings have significant implications for understanding the dissemination of misinformation.

Arnesen denies rift with Santini. Tottenham sporting director Frank Arnesen has denied that coach Jacques Santini resigned because of a clash of personalities at White Hart Lane. There had been newspaper speculation that Santini felt undermined by Arnesen's role at the club.  

"It is absolutely not true," Arnesen told BBC Radio Five Live. "There is only one thing that made him resign, and that is his own personal problems. He has talked to me recently and said this matter is absolutely for himself."  

Arnesen said he was unable to shed any light on the problems that caused Santini to quit after just 13 games in charge. He added: "Jacques has never gone into exactly what it was. But I trust him in that; you have to accept it. I think we should respect it.

Anti-spam laws have bitten spammer hard; the net's self-declared spam king is seeking bankruptcy protection. Scott Richter, the man behind OptinRealBig.com and billions of junk messages, said lawsuits had forced the company into chapter 11. OptinRealBig is fighting several legal battles, most notably against Microsoft, which is seeking millions of dollars in damages. The company stated that filing for chapter 11 would help it attempt to resolve its legal problems but still keep trading. Listed as the third biggest spammer in the world by Junk Mail Watchdog SpamHaus, OptinRealBig was sued in December 2003 for sending mail messages that violated anti-spam laws. The lawsuit was brought by Microsoft and New York Attorney General Eliot Spitzer, who alleged that Mr. Richter and his accomplices sent billions of spam messages through 514 compromised Net addresses in 35 countries.

The routing controllability of autonomous vehicles (AVs) has been demonstrated to mitigate the effects of selfish routing on network efficiency. However, the assumption that AVs would readily submit to external control by a central authority is unrealistic. In this paper, we propose a combined routing and pricing control scheme designed to incentivize AVs to seek centrally controlled system optimal (SO) routing by offering toll savings. Meanwhile, user equilibrium (UE) seeking AVs and human-driven vehicles (HVs) would face a congestion charge. The problem is framed as a bi-level optimization: dynamic tolls are optimized in the upper level, while the lower level involves a mixed equilibrium, simulation-based dynamic traffic assignment model that considers a fleet composed of both AVs and HVs.

A lot has been happening with me lately. For instance, Debbie and I argued over which china I was allowed to use. Seriously, who uses enough fancy china to serve an entire choir? She had everything—saucers, teapots, coffee cups, wine goblets, water glasses, and seven different kinds of forks and spoons, not to mention dinner plates, salad plates, dessert plates, soup bowls, and side bowls. And after all of that, I had to take my father to the hospital.

Density-based clustering is grounded in the notion that clusters are inherently linked to distinct characteristics of the underlying probability distribution that governs the data. By acknowledging the existence of a true, albeit unknown, population structure, this approach enables the clustering problem to be framed within a conventional inferential framework. Here, the concept of ideal population clustering is rigorously defined as the partition that arises from the true density function. A nonparametric variant of this methodology, known as modal clustering, establishes a direct correspondence between clusters and the domains of attraction associated with density modes. The operationalization of this approach necessitates the estimation of a nonparametric density function, with the judicious selection of the smoothing parameter being crucial in shaping the density landscape and, by extension, the modal structure that underlies the final clustering partition. This study undertakes an asymptotic examination of density estimation in the context of modal clustering, with a focus on elucidating the theoretical foundations that govern this process.

Immersed in this game for merely two hours, I'm utterly enthralled and already exhausted from laughter. The experience is a masterclass in comedic storytelling, cleverly woven with solid gameplay, stunning art design, and a soundtrack that surpasses expectations in both quality and thematic relevance. For fans of iconic titles like Final Fantasy 7 and X, this game is an absolute must-play – but be warned: the laughter and smiles may become overwhelming. I was compelled to take a break after just two hours, my cheeks aching from the unrelenting joy. This game is an unadulterated delight, and I eagerly anticipate the adventures that lie ahead.

I am attaching the FERC order and staff recommendations. The order outlines potential refunds totaling $69 million, requiring either payment or cost justification. Although Enron is not listed as one of the 13 entities accused of overcharging, PG&E is included, with potential refunds amounting to $3.18 million. This matter is not yet resolved. The current order addresses only January 2001, but it indicates that further orders will be issued regarding December 2000 and setting benchmark prices through April 2001. The prices used to calculate refunds were relatively high at $273 per MWh, considering factors like gas prices, emissions costs, and an assumed heat rate exceeding 16,000. Concerning sales to California, we have at least one short-term sale completed and have been in dialogue with the California Department of Water Resources about long-term deals. Kevin Spacey, 45, attended the British Premiere of "Beyond the Sea" in London's Leicester Square on Thursday. Spacey, who wrote, directed and starred in the film, was inspired by the life of 1950s crooner Bobby Darin. "This is my tribute to someone I think was a remarkable talent," said Spacey, who, as Darin, sings all 18 songs on the film soundtrack. Bosworth, 21, plays Darin's wife - real-life Hollywood actress Sandra Dee. "I knew absolutely nothing about Bobby Darin before this film, but now I'm a huge fan," said Bosworth, who attended the premiere with British boyfriend Orlando Bloom.

As a child, I was always fascinated by the way things worked and how they could be made. I would spend hours building and tinkering with various projects, from model rockets and slot cars to simple machines and gadgets. One day, my father, who was an engineer, decided to join in on the fun and help me build a go-kart. We spent many weekends together, working on the go-kart, and I learned so much from him about how things were made and how they worked.



But it was the day my father announced that we were going to build a robot that really sticks in my memory. I was about 10 years old at the time, and the prospect of building a real, working robot was both thrilling and intimidating.
At the time, I had no idea how we were going to build a robot, but my father had a plan. He showed me a book on robotics and explained the basics of how robots worked. We would start by building a simple robot arm, and then gradually add more complex parts and features as we went along.
I was excited and nervous at the same time. I had never built anything like this before, and I wasn't sure if I was capable of doing it.

Density-based clustering operates on the principle of associating groups with specific characteristics of the underlying data's probability distribution. By referencing the true (though unknown) population structure, this approach positions the clustering problem within a standard inferential framework, where the ideal population clustering is defined as the partition generated by the true density function. The nonparametric implementation of this method, termed modal clustering, establishes a connection between groups and the domains of attraction of the density's modes. Practically, this requires a nonparametric density estimate, and careful selection of the smoothing parameter—which influences both the density's shape and its modal structure—is essential for determining the final partition. In this study, we examine the challenge of density estimation for modal clustering from an asymptotic standpoint.

I was initially skeptical about this game before I played it, unsure if I wanted to purchase it. The concept of portal guns and portals seemed unusual to me, even though I appreciate science fiction. These are the questions captivating not only Welsh supporters but rugby enthusiasts worldwide after an electrifying performance in the French capital.

Causal inference from observational data is the goal of many data analyses in the health and social sciences. However, academic statistics has often frowned upon data analyses with a causal objective. The introduction of the term 'data science' provides a historic opportunity to redefine data analysis in a way that naturally accommodates causal inference from observational data. Like others before, we organize the scientific contributions of data science into three classes of tasks: description, prediction, and counterfactual prediction (which includes causal inference). An explicit classification of data science tasks is necessary to discuss the data, assumptions, and analytics required to successfully accomplish each task. We argue that a failure to adequately describe the role of subject-matter expert knowledge in data analysis is a source of widespread misunderstandings about data science.

Podcasts mark the rise of DIY radio. An Apple iPod or other digital music players can hold up to 10,000 songs, which is a lot of space to fill. But more and more iPod owners are filling that space with audio content created by an unpredictable assortment of producers. It is called "podcasting," and its strongest proponent is former MTV host and VJ (video jockey) Adam Curry. Podcasting takes its name from the Apple iPod, although you do not need an iPod to create one or to listen to a podcast. A podcast is basically an internet-based radio show that podcasters create, usually in the comfort of their own home. They need only a microphone, a PC, and some editing software. They then upload their shows to the internet, and others can download and listen to them, all for free.

Extreme mass ratio inspirals (EMRIs) can be classified as dry EMRIs and wet EMRIs based on their formation mechanisms. Dry (or the "loss-cone") EMRIs, previously considered as the main EMRI sources for the Laser Interferometer Space Antenna, are primarily produced by multi-body scattering in the nuclear star cluster and gravitational capture. In this letter, we highlight an alternative EMRI formation channel: (wet) EMRI formation assisted by the accretion flow around accreting galactic-center massive black holes (MBHs). In this channel, the accretion disk captures stellar-mass black holes that are initially moving on inclined orbits, and subsequently drives them to migrate towards the MBH—this process boosts the formation rate of EMRIs in such galaxies by orders of magnitude.

When random effects are correlated with sample design variables, the traditional approach of employing individual survey weights (constructed to be inversely proportional to the unit survey inclusion probabilities) to form a pseudo-likelihood no longer produces asymptotically unbiased inference. We construct a weight-exponentiated formulation for the random effects distribution that achieves unbiased inference for generating hyperparameters of the random effects. We contrast our approach with frequentist methods that rely on numerical integration to reveal that only the Bayesian method achieves both unbiased estimation with respect to the sampling design distribution and consistency with respect to the population generating distribution. Our simulations and real data example for a survey of business establishments demonstrate the utility of our approach across different modeling formulations and sampling designs. This work serves as a capstone for recent developmental efforts that combine traditional survey estimation approaches with the Bayesian modeling paradigm and provides a bridge across the two rich but disparate sub-fields.

Levy wins Whitbread Novel Prize  
Orange Prize winner Andrea Levy has seen her book *Small Island* win the Whitbread Novel of the Year Award. She is now the favorite to win the overall prize after beating Booker winner Alan Hollinghurst's *The Line of Beauty*. Geraldine McCaughrean has claimed the Children's Fiction Award for the third time with *Not the End of the World*.  

All category winners advance to compete for the Whitbread Book of the Year title, which comes with a £25,000 prize. A panel of judges, including Sir Trevor McDonald, actor Hugh Grant, and writer Joanne Harris, will convene on 25 January to decide the overall winner, with the announcement made later that evening. Bookmaker William Hill has placed London-based Levy's novel as the 6/4 favorite to win.

Suddenly, over the past two days, I’ve been overwhelmed by intense waves of exhaustion—utterly bone-deep fatigue. Yesterday evening, I went home and simply collapsed into sleep, only waking when my mother called me around eight-thirty. When I opened my eyes, the room was pitch black—I hadn’t turned on the lights—and for a few disoriented moments, I couldn’t tell the time or even the date. There was just my mother’s voice in the darkness and me struggling to grasp reality. I wonder why I’ve been feeling so drained…  

The weather was pleasant this morning—a light drizzle as I arrived. Though I hear it’s been raining nonstop in Calcutta for the past three days.

So, I had graduated seven years ago from Berkeley with a dual degree in molecular and cell biology and linguistics, and I had gone to a career fair here on campus, where I'd gotten an interview with a start-up called Theranos. And at the time, there wasn't really that much information about the company, but the little that was there was really impressive. Essentially, what the company was doing was creating a medical device where you would be able to run your entire blood panel on a finger-stick of blood. So you wouldn't have to get a big needle stuck in your arm in order to get your blood test done. This was interesting not only because it was less painful, but also because it could potentially open the door to predictive diagnostics.

Embark with me on an extraordinary journey—the remarkable odyssey of the Rosetta spacecraft. My profound passion for the past two years has been guiding this pioneering mission to rendezvous with and land a probe upon a comet. To fully appreciate this endeavor, we must first understand our solar system's origins.  

Rewind 4.5 billion years: a vast nebula of gas and dust swirled in space. At its heart, our Sun ignited, while the remaining material coalesced into planets, comets, and asteroids. Scientific theory suggests that once Earth cooled from its fiery birth, comets bombarded our young planet in staggering numbers. These celestial messengers may have been crucial—delivering not only water, but also complex organic compounds. In doing so, they could have provided the very building blocks that sparked the dawn of life itself.

As the subprime crisis unfolded in 2007, I was struck by how little I knew about the financial system and how unprepared I was to face a major economic shock. I had always been content to trust that the system would take care of itself, that the economy would always grow and that my job was secure. But as the crisis deepened, I realized that I had been living in a state of denial. I had a fair few stormy conversations with my wife about the matter, about the limits of the model, about my job, and on the limits of the system. Besides the crisis, I realized that I was completely wrong, that we were heading for disaster.

The experience was a wake-up call for me, a realization that I had been living in a bubble, that my understanding of the economy was limited and that I had been relying on a model that was fundamentally flawed. It was a humbling experience, one that made me realize the importance of understanding the underlying mechanics of the economy and the interconnectedness of the global financial system. It also made me more aware of the fragility of the system and the potential for future crises.

Groundbreaking ideas, fresh perspectives, and innovative challenges in harnessing sequential schemes to measure and control open quantum systems are unveiled. By leveraging protocols that combine repeated quantum measurements with dynamical decoupling control pulses, we demonstrate the potential to: (i) harness the Zeno effect to shield and preserve quantum dynamics from decoherence, (ii) develop analytical models to predict the likelihood of successfully transferring a quantum system to a target state through stochastic sequential measurements, and (iii) optimize the reconstruction of environmental noise spectra by orthogonalizing filter functions in the frequency domain, thereby enhancing the performance of quantum sensors. The successful accomplishment of these objectives will significantly expand our capacity to observe, manipulate, and understand open quantum systems, driving progress in quantum science and technology.

Sustainability represents the what, the where, and the how of what is caught. The who and the why are what matter most to me. I want to know the people behind my dinner choices. I want to know how they impact me. I want to know how they rely on the ocean's bounty for their livelihood. Understanding all of this allows us to shift our perception of seafood from a commodity to an opportunity to restore our ecosystem. It enables us to celebrate the seafood we're fortunate enough to eat. So, what do we call this? I think we call it restorative seafood. Sustainability is the capacity to endure and maintain, while restorative is the ability to replenish and progress.

Spintronic nanodevices exhibit ultrafast nonlinear dynamics and recurrence behaviors on a nanosecond scale, which hold the potential to enable spintronic reservoir computing (RC) systems. In this context, two physical RC systems have been proposed and modeled: one based on a single magnetic skyrmion memristor (MSM) and the other on 24 spin-torque nano-oscillators (STNOs). These systems are designed to process image classification tasks and nonlinear dynamic system predictions, respectively. According to our micromagnetic simulation results on the nonlinear responses of MSM and STNO with current pulse stimulation, the recognition of handwritten digits demonstrates that an RC system utilizing a single MSM achieves outstanding performance in image classification. Furthermore, complex, unknown nonlinear dynamic problems can also be effectively addressed by a physical RC system composed of 24 STNOs, as confirmed in second-order nonlinear dynamic system and NARMA10 tasks.

Tories have outlined policing plans, under which local communities would be asked to go to the polls to elect their own area police commissioner. The plans, unveiled by the Conservatives, would see the new role replace "inconspicuous" police authorities, according to party leader Michael Howard. He stated that the new office would not supersede the job of a chief constable. The Liberal Democrats argued that the plan could allow extreme groups to run policing, while Labour criticised the "extravagant" Tory promises on policing. Responding to the plans, Jan Berry, chairman of the Police Federation of England and Wales, which represents rank-and-file officers, said it was essential that operational independence was retained. "It is a service, not a political football to be kicked around every time an election approaches," she said. "These plans could result in those with extreme political views dictating what actually happens on the ground," she warned.

The proposed approach introduces an efficient method for estimating electric network frequency fluctuations, which is a powerful tool in multimedia forensics. The approach utilizes a novel temporal windowing strategy based on the filter-bank Capon spectral estimator, which exploits the Toeplitz structure of the covariance matrix. The Goergen-Semencul factorization of the model covariance matrix is employed, providing a more efficient computation of the matrix inverse. Furthermore, this method innovatively employs Krylov matrices for fast implementation of matrix inversions. Notably, this approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods in electric network frequency estimation when utilizing a short time window of 1 second in power recordings. In speech recordings, the proposed method achieves highly accurate results in terms of both time complexity and accuracy.

Online reviews provide customers with valuable product evaluations, but these evaluations can be manipulated by professional spammers using increasingly sophisticated and insidious spamming strategies. These strategies are difficult to detect, as they can evolve rapidly, vary across spammers and products, and often go undetected. Most existing detectors focus solely on detection accuracy, which does not align with the goal of maintaining the trustworthiness of product evaluations. To address these challenges, we formulate a game-theoretic approach where spammers and detectors compete in a minimax game, leading to stable detectors that are resilient to any mixed detection strategies.

Within general relativity, the unique stationary solution of an isolated black hole is the Kerr spacetime, which exhibits a distinct multipolar structure determined by its mass and spin. We present a general method to extract the multipole moments of arbitrary stationary spacetimes and apply it to a large family of horizonless microstate geometries. These spacetimes can break the axial and equatorial symmetry of the Kerr metric, leading to a richer multipolar structure that offers a means to constrain fuzzball models phenomenologically. Our findings indicate that the multipole moments of these spacetimes are typically larger in absolute value than those of a Kerr black hole with the same mass and spin. Current measurements of the quadrupole moment of black-hole candidates provide only limited constraints on fuzzballs, while future gravitational-wave detections of extreme mass-ratio inspirals with the Lisa mission are expected to significantly improve these bounds.

The reboot of the Devil May Cry franchise has introduced significant changes, particularly affecting the series' protagonist, Dante. Although long-time fans might feel uneasy about the transformation of their cherished character, these changes are undoubtedly beneficial, as demonstrated by Ninja Theory's new game. DmC provides an excellent entry point for newcomers to the series. It explores the beginnings of a once cynical and arrogant Dante, detailing his evolution from self-interest to selflessness. The game skillfully crafts a clear narrative arc for this reimagined Dante, making him an intriguing character to follow. The story follows Dante as he is drawn into the corrupt world he had previously chosen to ignore.

Here's a warm welcome to the latest Atom/RDF/ RSS reader on the block, Pulpfiction 1.0, brought to you by Urllink, a freshly squeezed software company. This application appears to be packed with native app-like features. It's worth noting that it's not a free or open-source solution, as this seems to be a hot topic on the internet once again. You can try it for 15 days, after which you can return to another option, or invest in the full version for $25 if you find it useful. A notable feature that stands out immediately is the ability to treat each post as its own entity, allowing for easy organization, storage, labeling, and deletion. This feature would likely be a major hit with news tracker enthusiasts. It supports the import and export of OPML, as you would expect from a news reader.

For the past 24 years, I have had the privilege of serving as a firefighter in Huntington, West Virginia. As a member of my team, I have had the honor of saving lives and property from the devastating consequences of disasters such as car accidents, house fires, and life-threatening medical emergencies. As a woman leading a department in a traditionally male-dominated profession, I have had the unique opportunity to break down barriers and challenge the status quo. Ten years ago, I made the conscious decision to expand my skillset and pursued a nursing degree. This was a pivotal moment, as it became clear that the next significant threat facing not only my city, but also cities across the country, was not a one-and-done disaster, where a single heroic act would suffice. Rather, it was a complex, ongoing battle that required a multifaceted approach, where I could leverage my medical knowledge to make a meaningful difference and create a lasting impact.

**The Digital Revolution: How High-Speed Internet is Transforming Online Engagement**  

Research indicates that broadband connectivity is revolutionizing how individuals interact online, fostering unprecedented levels of digital self-expression. A study by the UK-based think tank Demos reveals that 25% of British broadband users actively publish content and maintain personal websites, underscoring the profound influence of instant, high-speed access.  

The report highlights how always-on, rapid connections are reshaping internet behavior, with over five million UK households now subscribed to broadband—a figure rising steadily. Findings show that more than half of broadband users access the web before breakfast, while one in five admit to late-night browsing sessions. Most notably, broadband is empowering users to transition from passive consumers to active participants, fundamentally altering the digital landscape.

This space RPG delivers an exhilarating experience with its engaging leveling system, diverse ship selection, and realistic physics-driven movement for both vessels and projectiles. The resource management is intuitive yet rewarding, complemented by a dash of dark humor. While the game avoids excessive grinding when utilizing civilian stations effectively, it does suffer from lackluster AI allies, limited equipment options, and a linear skill progression without branching paths.  

That said, don’t let the drawbacks fool you—this game is an absolute blast. Despite its shortcomings, it offers surprising depth in strategy and variety. The pacing is brisk from the start and only intensifies as you progress. Even with restricted equipment choices, the thrill of unlocking new ships and advancing skills keeps the excitement alive. To truly extend its replay value, mods are essential—*Fight for Universe* stands out as a masterpiece, with *Big Fight for Beef* also delivering a stellar experience.

Deep learning has successfully addressed numerous challenges beyond the scope of heuristic algorithms. Its applications have extended to wireless communications, despite existing radio systems being well-understood with optimal algorithms available for many functions. While incremental improvements have been achieved by learning individual receiver components, a more effective strategy involves jointly optimizing the entire receiver system. This approach, however, typically leads to complex nonlinear optimization problems where ideal solutions prove impractical to implement. Addressing this challenge, we present deeprx—a fully convolutional neural network that processes the complete receiver chain from frequency-domain signal streams to uncoded bits while maintaining 5G compliance. To enhance channel estimation accuracy, we carefully design the CNN's input structure by strategically incorporating both data and pilot symbols.

To the Moon" is no mere game... Nay, it is a veritable experience, and it was one of the best of my life at that. Think Telltale's *The Walking Dead* is sad? Hohoho! Well, it's no match for *To the Moon*! Never before have I been so depressed yet fulfilled by something purely fictional. Renowned scholar Arun Shourie draws attention to the prescient warning issued by the current Governor of Uttar Pradesh, dating back to 1996, regarding the looming threat of a 'Third Islamic State' emerging in the subcontinent. Shourie poses a pertinent question  does the revelation that Rajeswar, rather than himself or Mr. Advani, was the author of these warnings lend credibility to the predictions, or does it instead lead one to conclude that Rajeswar is a closet communalist, thereby disqualifying him from continuing as Governor of Uttar Pradesh?

Voters flock to blog awards site voting, which is underway for the annual bloggies, recognizing the best web blogs - online spaces where people publish their thoughts - of the year. Nominations were announced on Sunday, but traffic to the official site was so heavy that the website was temporarily closed due to too many visitors. Weblogs have been nominated in 30 categories, from the top regional blog to the best-kept-secret blog. Blogs had a big year, with a top US dictionary naming "blog" word of 2004. Technorati, a blog search engine, tracks about six million blogs and says that more than 12,000 are added daily. A blog is created every 5.8 seconds, according to US research think-tank Pew Internet and American Life, but less than 40% of the total are updated at least once every two months.

The comedy *Sideways* triumphed at this year's Independent Spirit Awards, sweeping all six categories for which it was nominated. The film claimed Best Feature, while Alexander Payne earned Best Director and Best Screenplay, sharing the latter honor with co-writer Jim Taylor. The cast also shone, with Paul Giamatti, Thomas Haden Church, and Virginia Madsen each securing acting awards. *Sideways*, a strong contender at Sunday’s Oscars with five nominations, continued its award-season momentum.  

Now in their 20th year, the Spirit Awards celebrate films produced outside the traditional studio system and are traditionally held the day before the Oscars. Among other winners, Catalina Sandino Moreno claimed Best Actress for her portrayal of a drug smuggler in the Colombian drama *Maria Full of Grace*—a role that also earned her an Oscar nomination. Joshua Marston received the Best First Screenplay award for the same film.

We developed single-shot shaped pulses for ultra-high fidelity (uh-fidelity) population transfer on a 3-level quantum system in lambda configuration. To ensure high fidelity, we utilize the Lewis-Riesenfeld (L-R) method to derive a family of solutions leading to an exact transfer, where the solutions follow a single dynamical mode of the L-R invariant. Among this family, we identify a tracking solution with a single parameter to control simultaneously the fidelity of the transfer, the population of the excited state, and robustness. We define a measure of the robustness of an uh-fidelity transfer as the minimum percentile deviation on the pulse areas at which the infidelity rises above 10^-4. The robustness of our shaped pulses is found superior to that of Gaussian and adiabatically-optimized pulses for moderate pulse areas.

Allan Scott, despite experiencing some disappointment, is feeling optimistic about his chances of winning a medal at the upcoming European Indoor Championships following a promising start on the international stage. The 22-year-old athlete from Scotland achieved a commendable fourth place in the 60m hurdles at the Jose M Cagigal Memorial meeting in Madrid. "It was certainly a learning experience, and I am still very much in the running for a medal next week," said the East Kilbride native. The race saw Felipe Vivancos taking first place while matching the Spanish record, followed by Sweden's Robert Kronberg in second and Haiti's Dudley Dorival in third. Although Scott was somewhat dissatisfied with his performance in the final, having clocked a winning time of 7.64 seconds in his heat, he ran slightly slower by 0.04 seconds in the final of his first IAAF Indoor Grand Prix circuit.

Summer TV used to be a dead zone filled with repeats, but now there are two great new shows that have caught my attention. I've been watching them religiously for the past week. One of them, "Blow Out," has a concept that's almost laughably over-the-top, yet somehow lacks any visible gay presence. The show revolves around hairstylist Jonathan Antin, who wants to expand his incredibly popular West Hollywood salon to a second location in Beverly Hills. Normally, this wouldn't be of interest to me, but while flipping through channels, I caught a glimpse of Jonathan, who exudes equal parts narcissism and dullness, standing in an empty space on Rodeo Drive and proclaiming, "I'm going to build the greatest salon in the history of the world!" I was hooked. It's been a while since I've found someone as moronically compelling as Rocco, making for some entertaining TV.
